subsections.title: Cosmetic and Structural
subsections.comments: Boarded and disembarked vessel at night, unable to take proper pictures

questions.prompt: Condition of Shell Plating and Coating of External Hull, Top side and Boot Top
questions.comments: Boarded and disembarked vessel at night, unable to take proper pictures
questions.guidanceNotes:
Assess by Visual inspection, the Hull external paint condition & appearance of shipside from Fwd to Aft, above waterline - Top side & Boot top - if in good condition.

  • Check the condition of bulbous bow / bow area - for coating damages due to Anchor chain rubbing.
  • Check coating condition for rust stains and weepages, oil stains or cargo stains from deck scuppers and any other openings.
  • Check coating condition for rust stains and weepages from fish plate edges & from underside of fairlead rollers.
  • Check for any Oil Marks or any other marks on the External Hull / Near Hull Openings
  • Check Bulwarks, side rails, retention coamings or other structures - for stray paint arising out of recent hull painting in DD, which may be an eyesore.

General hull appearance - any horse ribbing. Note down dents & approx location / Frame numbers & dimensions of set in.

  • Check from outside - for signs of corrosion in way of the plating and welds (grooving corrosion), and for deformations, cracks, leakages. Note down locations
  • Check for damages and dents on Welded Fender bars fitted on Hull (if fitted)
  • Check for cracks & wastage on Bulwark / Fish plate / Spill retention coaming / Side rails - as visible from the outside.

questions.prompt: Condition of Fouling on Boot Top and Vertical sides
questions.comments: Boarded and disembarked vessel at night, unable to take proper pictures
questions.guidanceNotes: Assess for any Hull Fouling on Boot-top & Vertical sides - Acorn barnacles / Tubeworm / Gooseneck / Algae / Slime / Mussels / Calcareous / Others - % age extent of fouling / Draft lines of fouled area.

questions.prompt: Condition and Appearance of Super structure and Funnel
questions.comments: Boarded and disembarked vessel at night, unable to take proper pictures
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check Superstructure & Superstructure extensions seen from Jetty side / boat

General appearance of Bridge wing underside.

Any visible structural defects which warrant further investigation?

Check for Rust, stains & weeping marks.

Check funnel appearance and condition from Jetty side / boat.

Funnel Pipe stack colour and appearance.

Logo (if any) marked on funnel - condition / legibility.

subsections.title: Accm ladders, Pilot Ladders and Gangways

questions.prompt: Condition of Accommodation ladders, steps and safety nets well rigged.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check Accommodation ladder, gangways, pilot ladders/hoist and arrangement for safe boarding, in good condition. (A safety net should be installed in way of gangways where it is possible that a person may fall from the means of embarkation and disembarkation or between the ship and quayside.)

  • Check Safety nets for wear, damage, the effects of actinic degradation, and for the impregnation of chemicals, grease or paint. Safety net should be single and continuous. Multiple nets joined on board are not acceptable
  • Check accommodation ladder or gangway - clearly marked at each end with a plate showing the restrictions on safe operation and loading, including the maximum and minimum permitted design angles of inclination, design load and maximum load on bottom end plate. SWL / Max persons allowed.

Check all moving parts such as turntables, sheaves, tracks, bearings and rollers are free to turn and evidences of periodically greased.

  • Check gangway for distortion or cracks, corrosion particularly where aluminium ladders have mild steel fittings.
  • Check for Bent stanchions and side ropes inspected for abnormalities along their entire length.
  • Check underside of an accommodation ladder or gangway for condition, distortion, cracks and corrosion. Check Top platform supports and Bottom platform condition.
  • Check the structure of gangway supporting points and winch beds - inspected for distortion, cracks or corrosion. Check gangway winch brake & brake pads
  • Check Operation of control system of Gangway and limit switches & power supply system (electric/air motor)

Check if Portable ladders are in use, are they properly secured and at a safe angle?

  • Where in use - bulwark ladders are to be closely inspected to assess the condition.

Gangway wires to be renewed every Year

The condition of MOT ladders and Staunchions.

questions.prompt: Condition and Appearance of Forward Mast and Fittings and Stay Wire.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check that the forward mast and its fittings are in good working condition.

  • Check that the stay wires are in good condition and the bulldog clips are properly fitted.
  • Check that stay wires are not covered with protective sheathing and that wire is greased adequately.

questions.prompt: Condition of Aft Flag mast and stern light fittings
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check that Stern light and fittings are in good order

  • Check that Flag mast is in good order.

questions.prompt: Condition and appearance of Both Windlass, Anchors and Chains.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check that the anchor stowage condition is in order, that it makes 3-point contact on chaffing plate when fully housed in, and that flukes are in good shape and not bent, for both anchors.

  • Check the chain condition for any heavy wastage, cracks, tightness of studs, missing studs and for completeness of the equipment. (Studs are only welded on one side. Check no excessive gap on non-welded side)
  • Check condition of Anchor shackle, D-Shackle & Swivel for both anchors.

Check condition of bow stopper & locking pin, if contact is good & fits snugly - for both anchors.

  • Check condition of anchor lashing arrangements - lashing wires and shackles; for both anchors.

Check condition of deck flange for spurling pipe (bell mouth casting on deck) & guard. (No holes & corrosion); for both anchor chains.

  • Check condition of hawse pipe & deck flange of hawse pipe. (No holes, corrosion, or cracks); for both anchors. Check condition of safety guards / covers.

Check condition of Anchor hawse pipe bell mouth (horn or protuding haws/frogs eye - on ship side).

  • Check Anchor hawse fluke resting area. Check for any Hull indendations due to fluke. Check for Bent fluke.

. Check condition of Gypsy and the Cables are resting properly on the Gypsy.

questions.prompt: Condition and Readiness of deployment of ETA.
questions.guidanceNotes:
• For tankers, Check that the aft ETA arrangements in good order.

ETA manual available for ready reference at F'cstle.

  • For tankers, check that the emergency towing arrangement is clearly marked to facilitate safe and effective use even in darkness and poor visibility.
To check if max delpoyment time of fwd ETA (60 mins) and aft ETA (15 mins) is displayed at locations

questions.prompt: Condition and Operation of Windlass and mooring winch Machinery.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check Windlass machinery, Mooring machinery & Capstans in good condition: -

  • Brake bands in good condition; no abnormal wear. Check bottom brake band restrictor bolt position with necessary clearance.
  • Condition of brake drum surface.
  • Foundations, grating plates in good condition, no wastage or broken sections.
  • For Hydraulic Prime movers, Check Hydraulic lines & valves in good order. Check operation of isolation valves (if fitted)

For Electric prime movers, confirm controls and VFD systems are functioning properly.

  • No leakage of oil from windlass motor or gear box.
  • Save all / containment trays in good condition, no corrosion / knife edging.
  • Save all / containment trays - plugs with chain securing in place. No water or oil accumulation in save-alls.
  • Gear case oil level is visible and sight glass is clear ( where applicable). Check sight glass in good condition
  • Check clutch levers in good condition, with locking pins attached with chain.
  • Check no wastage or damage to claw coupling ends.
  • Check clear markings for Hoist & Lower directions available.
  • Check Gear Guard are in order. (not missing, no corrosion, or holes)
  • Check the grease nipples for windlass and winch operating shafts are in good condition, clearly marked and not painted over.
  • Check winches and windlasses fittings & hydraulic lines are in good condition and free of significant rust.

questions.prompt: Tounge bar Position and condition
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check the condition of Tounge Bar and wear down.

Check the condition of Hinged Point to check for any loose bolts or Cracks.

questions.prompt: Condition of Forward Mooring winch system
questions.guidanceNotes:
Forward Winches:

  • Operating condition of the winches and clutch.
  • Condition of the brake drum, brake lining, brake control & brake bands in good condition. (If the brake drum surface is corroded and rust flakes are visible, rotate the drum and assess the condition and analyse the further action to be taken.)
  • Check clearance/gap under bottom half of brake band is within limits.
  • Check that clear markings for Hoist & Lower directions available.
  • Check condition of storage drums, working drums & warping drums.
  • Safety gears for handling anchor station available for immediate use.
  • No leakage of oil from mooring winch motors.
  • Save all trays in good condition, no corrosion / knife edging.
  • Save all trays - plugs with chain securing in place.
  • Check clutch levers in good condition, with locking pins attached with chain.
  • Check Gear Guard are in order. (not missing, no corrosion, or holes)
  • Limits for brake tightening clearly marked.
  • Condition of the brake drum, brake lining, brake control & brake bands in good condition. (If the brake drum surface is corroded and rust flakes are visible, rotate the drum and assess the condition and analyse the further action to be taken.)

. Check the thickness of the Brake band and any need for renewal in the near future.

. Brake Rendering Tightness clearly marked.

questions.prompt: Condition and appearance of Fairlead, Bollards etc and SWL clearly marked
questions.guidanceNotes: Check the operating condition of fairleads, bollards, mooring bitts, and stand rollers, and that SWL is marked by bead welding. Check Fairleads & rollers are free & well-greased. Check condition of fairlead cap covers - not corroded or holed.

questions.prompt: Condition and Appearance of Mooring Lines/ Wires.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check the number and condition of mooring lines and towing lines.

Are the condition of mooring lines satisfactory and inspections records maintained as per MS&LMP ?

Check all mooring ropes and wires stowed neatly to minimise tripping hazards and mooring areas clear and unobstructed.

Are HMP ropes used and check the conditions.

questions.prompt: Condition and Operational Readiness of Bitter end arrangement.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check condition of bitter end arrangement & same should have unobstructed access

  • Check condition of chain locker manholes, manhole gasket conditions. Same battened down.
  • Check bitter-end knock off hammer in place, clearly identified and coloured (red)
  • Bulkheads (trans/longl.), longitudinal, transverse rings, horizontal girders, other members in good condition.

questions.guidanceNotes:
Spring Winches:

  • Limits for brake tightening clearly marked.
  • Condition of the brake drum, brake lining, brake control & brake bands in good condition. (If the brake drum surface is corroded and rust flakes are visible, rotate the drum and assess the condition and analyse the further action to be taken.)

questions.prompt: Condition of Aft ETA arrangement and procedure in place
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check that the AFT ETA is kept in ready to operate condition. Check that the ETA winch air motor is kept disconnected from the winch.

  • Check that the fire wire and other loose ropes are not obstructing the safe mooring operation.

questions.prompt: Condition of Aft Mooring winch system

Check Mooring machinery & Capstans for condition: -

  • Brake bands in good condition; no abnormal wear. Hinge pins and their retaining devices in good order.
  • Condition of brake drum surface.
  • Foundations, grating plates in good condition, no wastage or broken sections.
  • Check Hydraulic lines & valves in good order. Check operation of isolation valves (if fitted).
  • No leakage of oil from mooring winch motors.
  • Save all trays in good condition, no corrosion / knife edging.
  • Save all trays - plugs with chain securing in place.
  • Check clutch levers in good condition, with locking pins attached with chain.
  • Check Gear Guard are in order. (not missing, no corrosion, or holes)
  • Limits for brake tightening clearly marked.
  • Check condition of storage drums, working drums & warping drums.

Check the condition with particular attention to:

  • operating condition of the winches and clutch.
  • Condition of the brake drum, brake lining, brake control & brake bands in good condition. (If the brake drum surface is corroded and rust flakes are visible, rotate the drum and assess the condition and analyse the further action to be taken.)
  • Check clearance/gap under bottom half of brake band is within limits.
  • Check that clear markings for Hoist & Lower directions available.
  • Limits for brake tightening clearly marked.
  • Condition of the brake drum, brake lining, brake control & brake bands in good condition. (If the brake drum surface is corroded and rust flakes are visible, rotate the drum and assess the condition and analyse the further action to be taken.)

Check the thickness of the Brake band and any need for renewal in the near future.

Brake Rendering Tightness clearly marked.

questions.prompt: Condition and Operation of Fair lead rollers and operating freely
questions.guidanceNotes: Check the operating condition of fairleads, bollards, mooring bitts, and stand rollers, and that SWL is marked by bead welding. Check Fairleads & rollers are free & well-greased. Check condition of fairlead cap covers - not corroded or holed.

questions.prompt: Condition of mooring lines.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check the number and condition of mooring lines and towing lines.

. Are the condition of mooring lines satisfactory and inspections records maintained as per MS&LMP ?

questions.prompt: Tongue bar Position and condition
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check the condition of Tongue Bar and wear down.
Check the condition of Hinged Point to check for any loose bolts or Cracks.

questions.prompt: Condition and Operation of Fwd Horn
questions.guidanceNotes: Check condition of Foghorn, Deck lights, Steering lights on Fwd mast. Check platform and vertical ladder (Vertical ladders over 3 mtrs height must have safety hoops spaces max 900mm apart) for good condition

questions.prompt: Condition of Flag Staff, Suez canal Lights and fittings.
questions.guidanceNotes: Check condition of Flag staff, Suez Canal light fitting base, access ladder, landing platforms, chain guards, guard rails. Check condition of Suez Canal light fittings & box.

questions.prompt: Condition and Operation of Spraying machines, descaling machines etc
questions.guidanceNotes: Check condition of portable Electric & pneumatic tools, and condition of protective guards. Condition of electrical connections, hoses, insulations & joints. Confirm that no safety is bypassed.

questions.prompt: Deck Work shop ,Store area, condition of Tools, safety belts/harness , slings
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check that safety harnesses / belts are available for personnel working aloft.

Check that the store is clean and well-arranged and maintained.

  • Check if there are any unwanted articles & objects which need to be disposed.
  • Check if any garbage accumulated in Store areas.

Check that all the spares and store items inside the store room are kept safely against damage during rough sea - so as not to move, fall, collapse, or turn over due to ship's movement.

Check that all spaces are properly lit and that lighting equipment is maintained in satisfactory condition with a view to preventing electrical shocks.

  • Check all electrical cables trays paying particular attention to the insulation and sources of fire ignition.

questions.prompt: Condition and markings of all loose lifting equipment
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check Wire Slings & chain blocks well stored, maintained in good condition & SWL clearly marked.

  • Check that there is no damage, cracking or rust on chain blocks tackles and wire slings.
  • Check that all loose gear, pipes & plates in store and internal spaces are properly secured, so as not to move, fall, collapse, or turn over due to ship's movement.

questions.prompt: Condition and Appearance of Fwd Mooring area and Venitllations
questions.guidanceNotes:
General condition of Forward mooring area.

Check that the mooring area is painted with non-slip paint and any obstruction in the walk way is painted with tiger mark.

Check that all mooring ropes and wires are stowed neatly to minimise tripping hazards and mooring areas clear and unobstructed.

Check Booby Hatches condition for effective weathertightness, hatch coaming in good condition without heavy wastage or holes, condition of gaskets, counterweight arrangement, clamping devices all in good order.

Check that the Forecastle store ventilation arrangement is in order and no corrosion or damages existing.

  • Forecastle bell gong - Check availability and condition.

questions.prompt: Condition of Gratings and Railing on mooring area.
questions.guidanceNotes: Check the condition of Gratings and Mooring platform supports and Steps. Check the railing around mooring area.

questions.prompt: Condition and Appearance of Booby Hatch channels and Packings.
questions.guidanceNotes: Check Booby Hatches condition for effective weathertightness, hatch coaming in good condition without heavy wastage or holes, condition of gaskets, counterweight arrangement, clamping devices all in good order.

questions.prompt: Condition and Operation of Talk back system
questions.guidanceNotes: Check the satisfactory operation of the means of communication between the navigation bridge and Fore and Aft station (electric phones, public address system, talk back system).

questions.prompt: Condition and Operation of Bow thruster Unit.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check that Bow Thruster room is clearly identified & marked and in satisfactory condition, free of improperly stored heavy items.

Check that bow thruster room is ventilated & ventilation system (air damper, fan running condition) is in good order.

  • Check that the bow thruster room is well illuminated & emergency light (source from ESB) is in good order.

Check the motor/thruster shaft coupling condition. Grease points of couplings and motor clearly identified and marked, in good order. Check no signs of over greasing.

  • Check level of hydraulic system oil in the header tank. Sight glass in good order. No Hydraulic oil leakages. Hand pump / wing pump in good order.
  • Check that B/T main motor space heater is in good order & working.
  • Check B/T hydraulic control system power pack, hydraulic pumps and its motor condition. No oil leakages. Filter blockage indication in good order.
 Check that the B/T Electric starter & control panel is maintained in satisfactory condition, clean and well protected. Switches & controls identified & labelled. (identification / labelling / tags in place). Check lamp test condition. Confirm no loose cable connection or bypass jumpers.  Check that insulation mats are positioned OR insulation cement permanently laid up on the floor - in BOTH front & rear of switchboard & B/T panels. Electrical shock warning displayed on switchboard. Confirm that switchboard is protected from water spray.

questions.prompt: Header Tank Level, Condition of Gear Box Oil and last tested.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check that Bow Thruster room is clearly identified & marked and in satisfactory condition, free of improperly stored heavy items.

Check that bow thruster room is ventilated & ventilation system (air damper, fan running condition) is in good order.

  • Check that the bow thruster room is well illuminated & emergency light (source from ESB) is in good order.

Check the motor/thruster shaft coupling condition. Grease points of couplings and motor clearly identified and marked, in good order. Check no signs of over greasing.

  • Check level of hydraulic system oil in the header tank. Sight glass in good order. No Hydraulic oil leakages. Hand pump / wing pump in good order.
  • Check that B/T main motor space heater is in good order & working.
  • Check B/T hydraulic control system power pack, hydraulic pumps and its motor condition. No oil leakages. Filter blockage indication in good order.

questions.prompt: Condition and Appearances of water tight Fire control plan boxes
questions.guidanceNotes: Check the condition of Fire control plan Boxes kept out side the superstructure or at Manifold areas. Check the cap for the box ids free to open. Confirm the Fire control plan is clear and Legible and in good condition.

questions.prompt: Condition and appearance of Save all tray Coaming and drain plugs
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check the save all trays, inner side, edges etc. There should not be NO thinning down of tray top edges. Check the drain plug us secured correctly and sealing properly.

Free of oil, water & debris. Check Height of save all's are not greater than vent heads.  Check that plugs with chain on all containment trays are in place.

questions.prompt: Condition, Appearance and sealing of Side scuttles
questions.guidanceNotes: For vessel with Side scuttles, the sealing to ensured proper and can be closed properly avoiding water seeping inside to th ecabins.

questions.prompt: Condition and securing of Oxygen/ Acetylene bottles
questions.guidanceNotes:
Oxygen/ acetylene rooms should be clearly identified and marked with required warning signs.

Bottles to be properly secured. Empty bottles to be clearly marked.

The cylinders to be secured well and Caps in place.

questions.prompt: Condition and arrangement of Paint store.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Are Paint Store / locker rooms - clearly marked.

  • Has the fire hazard caution plate been displayed at the entrance.

Check that the weather tight doors are maintained well (no corrosion or holes), easy to operate, the packings are in order and not damaged / torn.

Check that the paint locker is maintained clean, and all the paints are stored, arranged properly and secured in the racks. No paint drums located directly below Sprinkler nozzle.

  • Check that there is no paint drum kept open.
  • Check that the required PPEs are kept and maintained clean and ready to use, including respiratory protective equipments - Filter masks, face shield, apron, gloves and eyewash.

Check if paint locker is used as dedicated Cargo / bunker sample locker: -

  • MSDS for cargo & bunker samples sighted.
  • Samples disposed after the storage period as recommended in Tech Manual / Cargo Manual.
  • Check cargo/bunker samples are properly arranged and securely stowed.

Adequate eye protection available for workshop machine tools.

  • Check that the Face shields are readily available near the machines.

Tool board for hand-tools organized, clean and well arranged.

  • Check that no broken tools are used and kept in the tool board.
  • Sufficient quantity of NON-SPARK tools available on deck (for tankers).

questions.prompt: Condition of Ex proof lightings and Ventillation systems.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check that the natural ventilation & Extraction system is working good order?

  • Check that the Ex. Proof light fittings are in good condition and no damages exist.
  • Check that there are no defects to the electrical cables or switches
  • Check that drain plugs for paint locker are free to open and cap fitted with chain (fitted on bulkhead outside)

questions.prompt: Overall condition and appearance of the bridge.
questions.guidanceNotes:
• Check housekeeping & general appearance of the Wheel house.
  • Assess the House keeping & operating condition of refrigerator & Hot water pot / Coffee maker?
  • Check garbage bins at wheelhouse - should be metallic and colour coded for segregation. They should not be overflowing.
  • Assess condition of Pilot chair and other upholstery items.

Assess condition of Bridge wing doors, glass and locking arrangement.

  • Check Bridge / Nav deck - Fire Door and closing arrangement and that No hold back hooks, ropes or wedge to keep open self-closing fire doors?
  • Condition of door, door frame and door hinge.

Cofee Pots and Glasses clean and well arranged and secured.

Air conditioning is comfortable.

questions.prompt: Condition and functioning of Standard Magnetic Compass
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check that standard compass is clearly readable by the helmsman at the main steering position.

  • Check the communication between the standard compass position and the main steering position.
  • Check that the table/curve of residual deviation (every year) is available and deviation record is maintained.
  • Check that the compass is free of bubbles & markings clear.
  • Check Compass illumination (both AC/DC) is in good working order.
  • If vessel's magnetic compass acts as a Transmitting magnetic compass (TMC) - check magnetic off course alarm - in good working order.

Check condition of spare magnetic compass - free of bubbles & markings clear. {Should be stored upside down to avoid wear of the needle bearing} - (not required if 2nd Gyro or SAT compass is available)

questions.prompt: Condition of spare magnetic compass.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check that standard compass is clearly readable by the helmsman at the main steering position.

  • Check the communication between the standard compass position and the main steering position.
  • Check that the table/curve of residual deviation (every year) is available and deviation record is maintained.
  • Check that the compass is free of bubbles & markings clear.
  • Check Compass illumination (both AC/DC) is in good working order.
  • If vessel's magnetic compass acts as a Transmitting magnetic compass (TMC) - check magnetic off course alarm - in good working order.

Check condition of spare magnetic compass - free of bubbles & markings clear. {Should be stored upside down to avoid wear of the needle bearing} - (not required if 2nd Gyro or SAT compass is available)

questions.prompt: Condition of Pelorus / Azimuth circle
questions.guidanceNotes: Check Pelorus / Azimuth circle (means for taking bearings) in good order.

questions.prompt: Condition and Operation of the Master gyro and All Gyro repeaters.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Checking the condition of the master gyro and gyro repeaters & all repeaters following Master gyro. Max allowable steady error is (+/-) 1.5 deg

  • Bridge centre repeater
  • Steering stand repeater
  • Bridge wing (Port) - repeater
  • Bridge wing (Stbd) - repeater
  • Master cabin / MCR (if fitted) - repeater
  • Steering gear room - repeater

Check the Master Gyro Annual service due date, if exceeded.

  • Gyro compass & magnetic compass errors regularly taken and recorded?
  • Check interchange switch between Gyro-1 to Gyro-2 (or TMC/SAT compass)

questions.prompt: Condition and Operation of course recorder
questions.guidanceNotes: Check functionality of Course recorder.

questions.prompt: Condition and Operation of Satellite compass
questions.guidanceNotes: Check functionality of Satellite compass (if fitted).

questions.prompt: Condition and Operation of X band Radar
questions.guidanceNotes:
X-band (3cm) - Check the operating condition of display (check LCD status also). Mention max range targets available. (For tankers, to be on Stby in port)

Check the performance monitor record. (For tankers, to be on Stby in port)

Check if the shadow sectors are marked (For tankers, to be on Stby in port)

Check the radar scanner condition - if any abnormal noise. (For tankers, to be on Stby in port)

Check the last renewal of Magnetrons. (Review magnetron running hrs)

Check the sensor feed - GPS, Gyro, AIS, Log - plus any other sensor if interfaced. (if any missing - ALARM status)

Check that the plotting facilities are available and in working order.

Check the operating condition of ARPA (X-band). (For tankers, to be on Stby in port)

questions.guidanceNotes:

Check that the plotting facilities are available and in working order.

Check the operating condition of ARPA (S-band). (For tankers, to be on Stby in port)

questions.prompt: Condition and functionality of Radar inter switching facility.
questions.guidanceNotes: Check functionality of radar interswitching, if available & in good order.

questions.prompt: Condition and Operation of Bridge Window wipers
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check that the Window Wipers are in good working order.

  • Check condition of Window wash nozzles, piping and isolating valve. (Piping not holed or corroded, nozzles free)
  • Check that the clear view screen is in good working order.Check its sealing arrangement specially on chemical Tankers.
  • Window heaters switching panel & verify window heaters working.

questions.prompt: Availability and Condition of minimum required Navigational COLREG shapes (Balls, Cylinder, Diamond)
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check the Fore & Aft masthead lights [on both main & emergency power - for in service (primary) and reserve (secondary) lights available]. Check procedure to check navigation light failure alarm available?

  • Check the side lights & black mat colour (not painted glossy).
  • Check the stern light.
  • Check the anchor light.
  • Check the NUC lights.
  • Check the distribution panel. Lamp test check for indications. Check the nav light console panel in good condition
  • Check spare lamps & fuses available in inventory.

Black ball, COLREG shapes - Check at least 3 sets are available & in satisfactory condition. (Black Diamon shape for tug boat, Black cylinder shape for deep draft ships)

Daylight signal, Morse light & Search light - check condition & in good order.

  • Daylight signal - testing on both 12/24 V circuit and batter (if provided)
  • Daylight signal - Check Spare bulbs (3 Nos) available.

Check Duty officer aware of availability of Forecastle bell gong. (Should be in forecastle)

questions.prompt: Condition and Operation of VDR
questions.guidanceNotes:
VDR: -

  • Physical status of system & components including bridge wing Mics.
  • Battery, Beacon Expiry date / validity (note down)
  • Availability of VDR playback software copy.
  • VDR interfaced with requisite sensors include ECDIS (both ECDIS) & both Radars.

Check that recording of VDR information are made continuouly and no alarm on VDR display panel on bridge exist.

  • Check that the copy of the annual certificate of compliance issued by approved testing or servicing facility is available on board.
  • Check that the keys, for the protection of Data collecting unit and hard disk (operating panel), are available and kept in a secure place.
  • Check documented procedure to retain & download VDR data in the event of an incident is available. Check Deck officers familiar with VDR data downloading & playback.

Condition and Operational Readiness of VDR including beacon Batteries, HRU, Float Free Capsule.

Check VDR fixed capsule & float free capsule for water ingress & no colour fading.

  • For Fixed capsule - check validity of Beacon battery
  • For Float Free Capsule - check HRU validity & FFC battery validity.

  • For Fixed capsule - check validity of Beacon battery
  • For Float Free Capsule - check HRU validity & FFC battery validity.

questions.prompt: Condition and Operation of Bridge equipments
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check the working condition, physical status & illuminations of bridge controls & indicators:-

Check that all the indicating lamps on bridge control console is in working condition.

Carry out lamp test and Check.

General checks on cleanliness and all cables have been secured properly.

  • External Alarm panels (UMS Ships)
  • Bridge Engine telegraph
  • Engine Alarm system
  • Telegraph data logger (if fitted)
  • Engine RPM indicator
  • Speed Log (for speed over water) - Distance indicator
  • Rudder angle indicator
  • Rate of turn indicator (if fitted for ships > 50000 GRT)
  • Checking ship's clock / Chronometer

questions.prompt: Condition and Operation of ship's whistle - Fwd& Aft
questions.guidanceNotes:
Ship's whistle -

Fwd - Check Sound signalling panel for operating condition.

Operation of Air Horn/Electric Horn.

Ship's whistle -

Aft - Check Sound signalling panel for operating condition.

Operation of Air Horn/Electric Horn.

questions.prompt: Condition of various Operational instruments and Navigation Accessories
questions.guidanceNotes: Check condition of Binocular(s), Barometer, Sextant(s), Manual fog signalling equipment, Loudhailer & Hand lead line.

questions.prompt: Condition of Computers/Printers and accessories
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check Computers, printers and accessories, including Power cables, and securing - condition & tidiness?

  • Check Company forms, manuals, vessel manuals & plans, Library - orgnaized and stored properly, with clear identification of files.

questions.prompt: Condition of Glasses, Port holes and Blinds.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check Condition of portholes, curtains & blinds.

Port hole glsses should not have any discolouration. Discolouration of the galsses have impact on Fire protection integrity.

questions.prompt: Condition and Opertional readiness of all Fire doors
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check Fire Door and closing arrangement. Confirm that No hold back hooks, ropes or wedges are in place to keep open self-closing fire doors

  • Check condition of door, door frame and door hinge.
  • Check Door locks functional and no handles missing.

Check Self closing doors closing tight.

For Magentic held Doors, confirm operational when any fire alarm sounds.

questions.prompt: Condition and Effectiveness of closing and sealing of Door frames.
questions.guidanceNotes:
For Tankers, check if accommodation space atmosphere being maintained at higher pressure than that of ambient air? *(AC systems must not be set be set at 100% circulation as this will cause the pressure the internal atmosphere to fall to less than that of external atmosphere, due to extraction fans in sanitary & galley spaces)

  • External air conditioning unit (eg. split AC) should not be operated during cargo/ballast/bunker operations unless certified as safe for use in presence of flammable vapours.

questions.prompt: Condition of Port Holes in public spaces and Cabins free of discolouration.
questions.guidanceNotes: Check whether the Cabin and Common space port hole Glasses have any kind of discolouration. If discoloured, it will affect the fire protection integrity.

questions.prompt: Conditions of lighting and fittings
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check condition of lighting and lighting shades, Air con supply & exhaust Louvers. Air dampers on air ducts to be clearly marked with IMO symbols.

  • Check that all the light fixtures supplied from emergency power source have been appropriately marked.

questions.prompt: Condition of overall housekeeping and cleanliness.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Ships office : Assess Housekeeping & operating condition of refrigerator, hot beverage pots & cold-water fountain?

  • Check condition of ships office conference table & chairs, Flooring, housekeeping in general, anti-roll securing arrangements of chairs?
  • Check condition of portholes, curtains & blinds.
  • Check garbage bins in ships-office - should be metallic and colour coded for segregation. They should not be overflowing.

questions.prompt: Condition of weather tight doors and its packings.
questions.guidanceNotes:
• Check Weather tight doors for each deck, ladder to accommodation external areas - anti-piracy arrangements adequate & available?
  • Are alleyways free of obstructions and exits clearly marked (IMO Symbols) that are legible and in good order?

questions.prompt: Condition, Appearance of all lights and their fittings including cable glands
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check the condition and operation of light fittings around superstructure. Ensure glasses are not faded. Cables and Glands well sealed and suports in good condition.

Emergency lights to be high lighted in RED and tested. Check the condition of Brackets holding the lights.

Check the condition and operation of Explosion prrof light fittings mostly fitted to Forward part of accomodation.

questions.prompt: Condition and effectiveness of explosion proof light fittings
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check Pump room lighting condition. Explosion proof fitting should be intact, tight and properly grounded and in good working order with no defects like cracked diffusers, makeshift repairs etc.

  • Check that Lighting / Ventilation interlock is working.

Check for isolation system of Power circuits and Explosion proof Lightings.

questions.prompt: Condition and Appearance of Engine room Skylights
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check that the skylights / machinery space opening - closing arrangements, including remote control and automatic means of closure when fitted, are operating satisfactorily. Sky light securing , not to miss any bolts for securing.

  • Check that the damper flaps / vent ducting are not corroded and integrity of the flaps is good.

questions.prompt: Condition and Cleanliness of Crew Cabins, Beds and Toilets
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check that crew cabins and mess rooms are kept in clean & habitable condition, are free from any infestation (roaches, rats, vermin & bedbugs).

  • Check that no stores, equipment stores, or cargoes are stowed in cabins or spaces, certified as crew cabins.

Check that the all the lights in the cabins are in good working order.

  • Check that there are Sufficient cloth hanging hooks fitted in cabin.
  • Check all the cabin cupboards, beds, bathrooms are in order and kept clean and hygiene
  • Check No Smoking in bed clearly posted near beds in cabins. STRICTLY No smoking in cabin.
  • Check that Garbage bins are Metallic, and colour coded correctly for segregation and that they are not overflowing?
  • Check that electrical features are in satisfactory order and that the electrical cables and cable connectors are maintained duly isolated to avoid risk of electrical shocks.
  • Check that the toilets are neat and clean, that flushes are working and not leaking, and that floor tiles are in good condition. Check that biodegradable detergents are in use.
  • Check Shower rooms, washbasins in good condition, clean and hot water available. Check Shower heads, taps and mixers in good condition. Where there is more than one toilet in a compartment, they are sufficiently screened to ensure privacy. Check in general Shower curtains in good condition
  • Check Hot water warning signs are clearly posted? Safety poster - CHECK WATER TEMP PRIOR ENTERING SHOWER TO AVOID SCALDING.

. Ash Trays of self extinguishing types.

questions.prompt: Cabin Air Conditioning temp maintained at Comfortable levels.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check that ventilation and heating systems (for voyages in cold climate) are in good operating order, including exhaust fans for sanitary facilities.

  • Check that the accommodation is not under negative pressure during cargo / tank cleaning operations when the Aircon is put in recirculation. (On tanker vessels)

questions.prompt: Condition and cleanliness of Public Toliets.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check that the accommodation spaces and corridors are properly lit, and the light shields are intact.

  • Check in the public WCs that hand wash and paper towels are available
  • Check the condition of the spaces and that bathrooms and sinks are neat and clean.

questions.prompt: Condition and Cleanliness of Laundry rooms and drier rooms.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check that the housekeeping in the laundry room is good.

  • Check for any non-working machines in the laundry. (If any machine is not working same to be isolated and marked accordingly)

Company posters pertaining to laudry well displayed.

  • Check Laundries are free of accumulations of clothing that could constitute a fire hazard? Check that no clothes are hung out to dry in the vicinity of the dryer room heater & that suitable warning notice is posted at location.
  • Check that hot water is available in the laundry.
  • Check Irons and ironing boards or their equivalent available and in good working order.

questions.prompt: Condition of Washing machines
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check that the housekeeping in the laundry room is good.

  • Check for any non-working machines in the laundry. (If any machine is not working same to be isolated and marked accordingly)

Company posters pertaining to laudry well displayed.

  • Check Laundries are free of accumulations of clothing that could constitute a fire hazard? Check that no clothes are hung out to dry in the vicinity of the dryer room heater & that suitable warning notice is posted at location.
  • Check that hot water is available in the laundry.
  • Check Irons and ironing boards or their equivalent available and in good working order.

questions.prompt: Condition of Ironing Boards and Iron.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check that the housekeeping in the laundry room is good.

  • Check for any non-working machines in the laundry. (If any machine is not working same to be isolated and marked accordingly)

Company posters pertaining to laudry well displayed.

  • Check Laundries are free of accumulations of clothing that could constitute a fire hazard? Check that no clothes are hung out to dry in the vicinity of the dryer room heater & that suitable warning notice is posted at location.
  • Check that hot water is available in the laundry.
  • Check Irons and ironing boards or their equivalent available and in good working order.

questions.prompt: Condition and Cleanliness of Recreation rooms.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check the Crew and Officer Recreation room for housekeeping, condition of chairs & other furniture's and cleanliness. NO OPEN type of ash trays.

Appearence and Cleanliness of Floors.

  • Check Crew & officers TV and other entertainment devices - in good working order.
  • Check UMS Alarm panels in smoke room, common spaces & cabins in good order.

Check if Smoking regulations are posted and being adhered to including prohibition of mechanical/portable lighters onboard.

Check if Self-extinguishing ashtrays (honeycomb, enclosed) in use in designated smoking areas. No ashtrays allowed in non-designated areas. (Smoking areas should be confined to locations within the accommodation and should not have doors or ports that open directly to open decks)

  • Safety matches or fixed (car-type) electrical cigarette lighters should be provided in approved smoking locations.Check if smoke extractors are available and is working.

questions.prompt: Condition of Furniture and securing arragements
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check the Crew and Officer Recreation room for housekeeping, condition of chairs & other furniture's and cleanliness. NO OPEN type of ash trays.

Appearence and Cleanliness of Floors.

  • Check Crew & officers TV and other entertainment devices - in good working order.
  • Check UMS Alarm panels in smoke room, common spaces & cabins in good order.

Check if Smoking regulations are posted and being adhered to including prohibition of mechanical/portable lighters onboard.

Check if Self-extinguishing ashtrays (honeycomb, enclosed) in use in designated smoking areas. No ashtrays allowed in non-designated areas. (Smoking areas should be confined to locations within the accommodation and should not have doors or ports that open directly to open decks)

  • Safety matches or fixed (car-type) electrical cigarette lighters should be provided in approved smoking locations.Check if smoke extractors are available and is working.

questions.prompt: Condition and Operation of Water fountains.
questions.guidanceNotes: Check that drinking water fountains if fitted in the alley ways, are in good working order.

questions.prompt: Condition and availability of Gym equipments.
questions.guidanceNotes: Crew Recreation room & Gymnasiums (if provided) - Check all equipment's are well secured and in good order. Lighting and ventilation in good order. Check that space is not being used as a storage area

questions.prompt: Condition and House keeping of Changing Rooms.
questions.guidanceNotes: Check Engineer's / Rating's change room's - housekeeping and hygiene condition. No broken lockers. No accumulation of oily rags, tools, dirty shoes, oily/greased boiler suits & other clothing. Check other amenities - such as chair, bench, wash basin mirror etc.

questions.prompt: Condition and House keeping of Suez Canal rooms.
questions.guidanceNotes: Suez Canal room - housekeeping and hygiene condition. No broken furniture's & beds. Check other amenities - such as chairs, table, wash basin mirror etc. No accumulation of oily rags, tools, dirty shoes, oily/greased boiler suits & other clothing.

questions.prompt: Condition and Operation of the Magnetic compass
questions.guidanceNotes:
Physical Inspection - Check presence of bubbles / liquid leakage.

  • If vessel's magnetic compass acts as a Transmitting magnetic compass (TMC) on records - Check that magnetic sonde/flux gate is fitted in magnetic compass (base) & physical condition.
  • Check base of magnetic compass stand. Signs of leakage / cement box?

questions.prompt: Condition of Compass base support stand
questions.guidanceNotes:
Physical Inspection - Check presence of bubbles / liquid leakage.

  • If vessel's magnetic compass acts as a Transmitting magnetic compass (TMC) on records - Check that magnetic sonde/flux gate is fitted in magnetic compass (base) & physical condition.
  • Check base of magnetic compass stand. Signs of leakage / cement box?

questions.prompt: Condition and Appearance of Monkey island
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check that the dangerous area has been marked clearly on the deck around the antennae

  • the antennae and other fittings on the Monkey Island have been identified clearly & stencilled neatly.
  • the electrical junction boxes and other electrical fittings are maintained well and not rusted / broken
  • the electric cables on the monkey island are in good condition and the cable glands are maintained well and laid properly.

Check for antenna plan - available & updated.

Using antenna plan - check & confirm physical status of antenna & connectors & cables.

  • MF HF - Tx Antenna & DSCX Antenna - Whip
  • GPS Antenna
  • AIS - GPS Antenna & VHF Antenna (whips AE) / combined antenna.
  • VHF No.1 Tx Antenna, DSC Antenna
  • VHF No.2 Tx Antenna, DSC Antenna
  • SSAS Antenna
  • Navtex Antenna & pre-amp unit
  • SAT-C Antenna
  • LRIT Antenna
  • FBB Antenna
  • Bearing repeater (if fitted)
  • Check that antennae are in satisfactory condition without damage or missing components.

questions.prompt: Condition of Main/antenna mast, brackets, stays, ladders & fall preventer rings.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check Antenna masts and brackets are in good condition, without heavy corrosion or wastage.

  • Check condition, securing and earthing of V-SAT antenna

questions.prompt: Condition and Appearance of Main Mast.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check Navigation light for correct functionality.

  • Check that the light fittings, Radar Scanners and Air whistle are in good working order

Mast fitted antenna - as per antenna plan check correctness & physical status of connectors - include loose wires, corroded base plates / brackets for:-

  • X-band radar
  • S-band radar
  • Satellite antenna Sat-C
  • Wind speed indicator
  • Any other antenna, if fitted.

Check that the Main Mast is maintained rust free & coating condition maintained. Check condition of platform and vertical ladder (Vertical ladders over 3 mtrs height must have safety hoops spaces max 900mm apart) are in good condition

  • Check that the mast stays are well maintained and properly secured. The direction of Bulldog clips must be checked.
  • Check Stay wires are not covered by protective sheathing and wires greased adequately.

questions.prompt: Condition of electrical cables, Navigation Lights fittings, Stay wire etc
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check Navigation light for correct functionality.

  • Check that the light fittings, Radar Scanners and Air whistle are in good working order

Mast fitted antenna - as per antenna plan check correctness & physical status of connectors - include loose wires, corroded base plates / brackets for:-

  • X-band radar
  • S-band radar
  • Satellite antenna Sat-C
  • Wind speed indicator
  • Any other antenna, if fitted.

Check that the Main Mast is maintained rust free & coating condition maintained. Check condition of platform and vertical ladder (Vertical ladders over 3 mtrs height must have safety hoops spaces max 900mm apart) are in good condition

  • Check that the mast stays are well maintained and properly secured. The direction of Bulldog clips must be checked.
  • Check Stay wires are not covered by protective sheathing and wires greased adequately.

questions.prompt: Functioning and marking of ventilators, Condition of Deck Penetrations.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check Ventilators are good condition, clearly marked with open/close markings, free to operate, wire mesh intact & sealing effective.

  • Check gooseneck ventilators, flap & hinge, rubber sealing and wire mesh condition.
  • Check that the Open / Close position of the vent flaps of vent heads are clearly marked.
  • If the Mushroom vents have the rod indicators for the Close / Open position, check that the indication is exactly matching with the position of flaps and the rods are not damaged / cut.Check for deck penetrations & sealing condition - corrosion wastage of penetration stub pipes.
  • Check No unauthorised penetrations in monkey island and wheelhouse.
  • Check condition of deck drains, drain covers and scupper pipes - for blockages and wastage.
  • Check the handrails on the monkey Island deck are intact.

questions.prompt: Condition, Appearance and securing of Ships Name Board
questions.guidanceNotes: Check Ship's name board - cosmetic condition & securing.

questions.prompt: Condition and Appearance of External Super Structure.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check the Condition and Appearance of Super structure from all sides.

Check for Rust weeping marks from Sides including Ventillator hinges, gunwales, and structure welds.

Check the condition of Port holes exterior and weeping marks. All rust weeping marks to be attended on priority.

Check if any discolouration is seen from outside for the port hole glasses.

Condition of underside of Bridge wings.

Condition of Vents and louvers around accommodation external. All ventillations and openings to be identified to the space it serves. Open/Close markings toi be legible. Hinges/Pins well greased.

Condition of Pipe lines and Supports.

Checked whether the ladders and steps are in good condition (not corroded / thinned down) and well painted.

Condition of scuppers and Pipes.

Condition of weather Tight Doors and its channels and Packings. The Door locks to be well greased for easy operation.

HRA security locks to be insuch a way that it doesnot compromise on safety of personnel and ease of rescue.

questions.prompt: Condition and Adequacy of Personal Protective Equipment .
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check that the Battery room is clearly identified & marked at the entrance. Check No Smoking warning sign posted.

Check that the battery room ventilation is in open condition and identified by stencilling. Room must be dry & well ventilated.

  • Check internal condition of natural mushroom vent from battery room side

Check that the PPEs required (rubber gloves & goggles) in the battery room are kept in a easily accessible place and kept clean. Check the validity of eye wash. Rubber mats to be ensured provided in place.

Check Battery room lighting condition & check if Explosion proof fitting, they are intact and gas tight.

  • Check that general condition of electrical equipment, including conduits and wiring is satisfactory

questions.prompt: Condition of Ex proof light fittings in battery room.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check that the Battery room is clearly identified & marked at the entrance. Check No Smoking warning sign posted.

Check that the battery room ventilation is in open condition and identified by stencilling. Room must be dry & well ventilated.

  • Check internal condition of natural mushroom vent from battery room side

Check that the PPEs required (rubber gloves & goggles) in the battery room are kept in a easily accessible place and kept clean. Check the validity of eye wash. Rubber mats to be ensured provided in place.

Check Battery room lighting condition & check if Explosion proof fitting, they are intact and gas tight.

  • Check that general condition of electrical equipment, including conduits and wiring is satisfactory

questions.prompt: Condition of Emergency Batteries and GMDSS Batteries and connections
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check that the batteries are stored in good condition as a result of Physical appearance, secure & separated from each other, clearly identified.

  • Check that the Battery charger & rectifier unit panel is in good order and there is no low insulation on the Battery charging system.
  • Checking that batteries are in good condition and fully charged viewing the result of measuring specific gravity of acid, liquid level and terminal voltage. In case of maintenance free battery, check that expiry date is available.
  • Check that the cables, terminal connections are in good condition (no dirt) and the terminals are coated with petroleum jelly to prevent corrosion. Confirm no build up of salt. Check that gas vents of battery are free & clear. Confirm no deformation of battery casings (bulging due to gas build-up).

. Load test of Batteries to be done during sailing VIR

  • Check the last renewal of battery set.Both Emergency lighting and GMDSS battery (Batteries to be renewed every 3 years)

Check that the battery log is being maintained

Check electrolyte & distilled water inventory is sufficient and in good order.

questions.prompt: Condition and House keeping of Galley Interior
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check that the door from pantry to galley is not lashed open with hold back arrangements, and door closers closing properly.

  • Check the galley drains are clear and no water stagnation.
  • Check galley air con is working in good condition.
  • Check that all the hot plate ranges are working in good condition.
  • Check that the filter screen of hot plate exhaust / range hoods is clean and no oil traces in the screen. Check oil drain is clear without any clogging.

Check those galleys, pantries and food preparation areas are clean and free of any infestation with no blocked drain, damaged flooring or tiling.

  • Check that cleaning substances are stored away from food.
  • Check that working utensils are clean
  • Check whether all the equipment's available in Galley are in good working order and maintained clean.
  • Check that grease trap filters are cleaned regularly. Records available and posted.

Check Pollution placard available in the galley.

questions.prompt: Condition and Operation of Galley AC.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check that the door from pantry to galley is not lashed open with hold back arrangements, and door closers closing properly.

  • Check the galley drains are clear and no water stagnation.
  • Check galley air con is working in good condition.
  • Check that all the hot plate ranges are working in good condition.
  • Check that the filter screen of hot plate exhaust / range hoods is clean and no oil traces in the screen. Check oil drain is clear without any clogging.

Check those galleys, pantries and food preparation areas are clean and free of any infestation with no blocked drain, damaged flooring or tiling.

  • Check that cleaning substances are stored away from food.
  • Check that working utensils are clean
  • Check whether all the equipment's available in Galley are in good working order and maintained clean.
  • Check that grease trap filters are cleaned regularly. Records available and posted.

Check Pollution placard available in the galley.

questions.prompt: Condition of Filter screens and Last clean Date.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check that the door from pantry to galley is not lashed open with hold back arrangements, and door closers closing properly.

  • Check the galley drains are clear and no water stagnation.
  • Check galley air con is working in good condition.
  • Check that all the hot plate ranges are working in good condition.
  • Check that the filter screen of hot plate exhaust / range hoods is clean and no oil traces in the screen. Check oil drain is clear without any clogging.

Check those galleys, pantries and food preparation areas are clean and free of any infestation with no blocked drain, damaged flooring or tiling.

  • Check that cleaning substances are stored away from food.
  • Check that working utensils are clean
  • Check whether all the equipment's available in Galley are in good working order and maintained clean.
  • Check that grease trap filters are cleaned regularly. Records available and posted.

Check Pollution placard available in the galley.

questions.prompt: Condition and Operational Readiness of galley exhaust and ventilator flaps.
questions.guidanceNotes: Check that galley exhaust ventilator flaps are freely moving and are properly sealing while closed.

questions.prompt: Condition and Cleanliness of Mess rooms.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check that the mess rooms housekeeping and cleanliness is in order, the fridges are kept clean & regularly defrosted. Check hot beverage & cold-water facilities.

  • Check that the equipment's available are in good working order and there is adequate insulation resistance in the equipment's.

questions.prompt: Condition, Cleanliness and Operating temperatures of cold rooms
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check that Cold room temperatures are being maintained. Defrosting arrangements working. Check door sill heaters in good working order & no ice formation around cold room door packings.

  • Check that Anti-skid mats are available in cold room & in good order.
  • Check that the evaporator coil is maintained well and there is no heavy ice formation.
  • Check that Cold room, door unlock system in good order.
  • Check that Cold room alarm bell (emergency call system for being locked in) & call point lights is in good working condition.
  • Check that provision storeroom and refrigerated storerooms are in good condition, and free of any insect infestation. Refrigerated provision storerooms to be at a suitable temperature.
  • Conduct random checks for any expired provisions.
  • Check that vessel has enough food and drinking water to reach next port

questions.prompt: Condition and House keeping of Hospital.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check that hospital and sick bay are clean and free of any infestation and that equipment and instruments are clean and orderly.

  • Check and confirming that the hospital is not used as an extra-accommodation room and that doors can close properly.
  • Check Hospital Call alarm to bridge is in working condition.
  • Check sanitary facilities provided for hospital - condition toilet, washbasin, tube or shower - in good condition.
  • If hospital has direct opening on to the weather deck, check the weather tight door, whether they are maintained well (no corrosion or holes), easy to operate, the packings are in order and damaged / torn.

questions.prompt: Condition and Cleanliness of Hospital Toilet, wash basin and supply of Hot water.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check that hospital and sick bay are clean and free of any infestation and that equipment and instruments are clean and orderly.

  • Check and confirming that the hospital is not used as an extra-accommodation room and that doors can close properly.
  • Check Hospital Call alarm to bridge is in working condition.
  • Check sanitary facilities provided for hospital - condition toilet, washbasin, tube or shower - in good condition.
  • If hospital has direct opening on to the weather deck, check the weather tight door, whether they are maintained well (no corrosion or holes), easy to operate, the packings are in order and damaged / torn.

questions.prompt: Condition and Operational Readiness of Medical Oxygen and Resuscitator.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check Vessels possess all medicine as per flag and company requirement.

  • Random check of medicines for any expired.
  • Check if controlled drugs / narcotics are stored in the custody of the Master & if up-to-date inventory available?
  • Check that vessel has suitable arrangement for disposing the used syringes.
  • Check that vessel has sufficient medical bags for disposing the used medicines.
  • For ships carrying dangerous goods, checking those suitable antidotes for the cargoes being carried are available in sufficient number for the relevant cargoes.

questions.prompt: External condition of funnel for the logo, pipe stack colour and funnel flaps and free of Oil stains
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check funnel appearance and condition, pipe stack colour and appearance. Logo if any marked on funnel - condition / legibility.

  • Check Funnel flaps - local closing mechanism, free to operate & flaps close fully. (Light check)

Check Lighting & light fitting condition of Funnel deck.

  • Check projector lighting & light fitting condition; for Funnel logo.

questions.prompt: Condition of the light fittings for funnel.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check funnel appearance and condition, pipe stack colour and appearance. Logo if any marked on funnel - condition / legibility.

  • Check Funnel flaps - local closing mechanism, free to operate & flaps close fully. (Light check)

Check Lighting & light fitting condition of Funnel deck.

  • Check projector lighting & light fitting condition; for Funnel logo.

questions.prompt: Condition of weather tight door on funnel deck.
questions.guidanceNotes: Check that the weather tight doors on the Funnel deck are maintained well (no corrosion or holes), easy to operate, the packings are in order and not damaged / torn.

questions.prompt: Condition of ladders, deck plating and drains on funnel.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check that the ladders around the funnel superstructure are maintained well, the steps and the handrails are in good condition. (No corrosion & holes, no doublers)

  • Check Safety chains on the ship's side are in good order - no links missing / damaged / corroded?
  • Check that the deck plating around the funnel superstructure is well maintained/painted and there is no rust / corrosion.
  • Check condition of deck drains, drain covers and scupper pipes for blockages and wastage.

Check the Hanging rails for rigging bosun chairs on funnel in good condition. (no corrosion and wastage.

  • Check the condition of funnel top / pipe stack deck. Check that the scupper drains are clear and not blocked with soot/debris. Check for any crack around all vents.
  • Check Exhaust pipe spark arrestor mesh condition for M/E, A/E, Boilers & IG - should be intact. (for Tankers)

questions.prompt: Condition of save all tray and Filling lines around the funnel.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check the save all trays around the funnel/accommodation are in good order (not wasted) and not used for storing any material. Check that no water is accumulated in the save-all trays

Check that the Emgy generator filling manifold is in good condition and all the lines are identified and bead welding of identification visible on the flanges.

questions.prompt: Condition of portable radios, VHF unit in CCR including charging stations.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check Total number of working portable radios, holsters and charging stations in good order. Any Defective ones to be followed up.

Check VHF & UHF radio equipment in CCR are in good condition & operational. Are switched to low power setting during cargo operations.

  • Check portable radios (Intrinsically safe), holsters and charging stations in good order & not compromised on Intrinsic safety. Defective ones to be followed up

questions.prompt: Condition and Operational readiness of Em Generator Engine.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Confirm Emergency Generator kept clean, free of oil and necessary operating procedures both for Switch board side and Engine side well displayed.

Check that Emergency Generator room is clearly identified & No unauthorized entry marked on the entrance door

  • Check that the compartment is kept in satisfactory condition of cleanliness, ventilation and that no materials are improperly stored inside.
  • Check Lighting arrangements of the room and switchboard panel.
  • Test the emergency diesel generator starting in both manual and automatic modes. Check if capable of coming onload automatically within 45 seconds .
  • Run the engine on no load (for at least 20 minutes) and check the Switch board meters and make sure all are in good working condition
  • Check for proper operation of engine instruments: rpm, oil pressure, temperature, amps.
  • Check that there is no low insulation on the Emcy switch board, distribution & feeder panels.
  • Couple the emergency generator on the emergency switchboard, check the operation & check that all emergency functions are satisfactory.
  • Transitional source of power & emergency power batteries checked for operation.
  • Has the emergency generator recently tested on full load without any problems.

questions.prompt: Condition and appearance of Vent Louvers and flaps.
questions.guidanceNotes:
• Check the Emergency Generator room vent flaps are in good condition and proper poster is displayed to open the same before operation of the engine
  • Check suction louvre & bellows for the engine radiator cooling fan.
  • Check condition of exhaust piping and freeness of self-closing flaps (if fitted). For tankers, check exhaust pipe spark arrestor mesh condition.
  • Emgy Gentr room ventilators WITHOUT closing arrangements - has been an issue with many PSCs. If such is the case - note as defect/work order.

questions.prompt: Condition of Radiator and Cooling water system.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check that there is no leakage present in any of the engine system / pipelines in the Emcy Generator Room

  • Check that radiator water is maintained at required level and anti-freeze is dosed.
  • Physically check the Radiator fins condition (many vessels have been noted with fins disintegrated leading to issues with PSCs)

questions.prompt: Condition and Operational readiness of Starting systems
questions.guidanceNotes: Check the primary means fo Starting system. If its by battery, do the trials multiple times inorder to confirm th ecapacity of Battery is sufficient for Multiple starts.

questions.prompt: Condition of Batteries and last renewal date.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check that batteries are in good condition as a result of measuring specific gravity of acid, liquid level and terminal voltage. In case of maintenance free battery, check that expiry date is available.

  • Check the last renewal of starting battery set. (Batteries to be renewed every 2 years)
  • Check that the cables, terminal connections are in good condition (no dirt) and the terminals are coated with petroleum jelly to prevent corrosion. There should be no build-up of salt. Check that gas vents of battery are free & clear. No deformation of battery casings (bulging due to gas build-up).
  • Check those starting batteries for Emgy Gentr are fully charged OR Primary means of starting in good order.

questions.prompt: Condition and Operational readines of second means of start.
questions.guidanceNotes:
• Check that the secondary means (for 2 independent means) for engine starting is in good working condition.

If the use of Spring loaded cranking system is used, Check th econdition of spring system and also try to keep spares.

questions.prompt: Condition of Shore connection System.
questions.guidanceNotes:
• Check condition of Shore connection panel & lighting.
  • Check condition of bulkhead penetration arrangement for shore connection cable.

questions.prompt: Condition and Operational readiness of Fire extinguishers inside Em gen room.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Portable fire extinguishers (foam, dry-powder, CO2) - check condition of container/cylinder for corrosion / serious damage. Instructions for use should be legible.

  • Check Portable extinguishers - operating instructions clearly marked.
  • Verify last inspection by vessel as per PMS and shore servicing dates.

questions.prompt: Condition and Operation of Emergency Air Compressor .
questions.guidanceNotes:
Operation of Em'cy Air compressor & condition of reserviors

Reducing pressure valves and gauges for compressed air system in good order?

Recovery from DEAD Ship to be simulated.

Draing of Emergency Air Bottle done regularly.

Where ever Hand Pumpe Compressors are provided, try filling the Emergency Air bottle inorder to be able to start a Generator Engine.

subsections.title: Emergency Bilge suction in Engine room

questions.prompt: Condition and Operational Readiness of Em Fire Pump.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check physical condition of pump, prime mover foundation, gland seal / mechanical seal area - for leakage and corrosion.

  • Check FW connections or alternate connection of water supply at emergency fire pump
  • Check that the priming unit header tank has been kept filled up with water, including physical condition of Auto check valve, piping arrangement of priming unit & disengaging mechanism of primer unit. If fitted, check the electric driven vaccum pump fit for purpose and check the starting sequence timing
  • Check condition of the starting devices (Starter Panel).

. For Em fire pump in Steering gear compartment, ensure availabilty of starting system out side steering gear compartment. ( During HRA transit time, possibility of Locking steering gear flat may exist and this leads to non availability of starting system if no start system out side steering gear compartment.)

Check Guard rails, safety chain and access trunk to Emergency Fire pump room, including vertical ladder.

  • Check Lighting condition of Emergency fire pump room.
  • Check that the Emergency fire pump room location is clearly identified & marked with Luminous IMO symbol + Poster and access is clear without any obstruction.
  • Check that the operating procedure is available near the pump location / operating panel
  • Check that the pipe lines and valves are in good condition without corrosion / rust. Valves locked in OPEN position - except of automatic valves, where check for autooperation & controller. Check that fire hydrants are free of leaks and easy to operate

. Check that the Emcy fire pump space bilge high level alarm is working good.

  • Check the bilge draining line has been provided with spring loaded valve (located in E/R) and it is maintained in good condition.

Try out Remote starting of emergency fire pump.

o Start the pump with hoses rigged fwd & aft, and check that the priming unit is engaging initially and disengages once the pressure is built up in the line.

o Check that there is no leakage either from the pump side or on the priming unit side.

o Check SUCTION pressure & the DISCHARGE pressure developed is normal and within short time.

o For Vessels where hydraulic operation of the suction valve is provided: Operate the valve and check the funtioning is proper. Check that the correct operating procedure is displayed for the suction valve.

o Run test of EFP for 10 minutes.

o Verify that the relief valves are free of leaks and that water do not flow through them.

If the Emergency Fire pump is located forward and is located at th ebottom, then Means of escpae from the Emergency fire pump room bottom and also to lift any injured person to be provided. Check the arrangement and confirm it satisfies the requirement.
