  • Check that the Emergency fire pump room location is clearly identified & marked with Luminous IMO symbol + Poster and access is clear without any obstruction.
  • Check that the operating procedure is available near the pump location / operating panel
  • Check that the pipe lines and valves are in good condition without corrosion / rust. Valves locked in OPEN position - except of automatic valves, where check for autooperation & controller. Check that fire hydrants are free of leaks and easy to operate

. Check that the Emcy fire pump space bilge high level alarm is working good.

  • Check the bilge draining line has been provided with spring loaded valve (located in E/R) and it is maintained in good condition.

Try out Remote starting of emergency fire pump.

o Start the pump with hoses rigged fwd & aft, and check that the priming unit is engaging initially and disengages once the pressure is built up in the line.

o Check that there is no leakage either from the pump side or on the priming unit side.

o Check SUCTION pressure & the DISCHARGE pressure developed is normal and within short time.

o For Vessels where hydraulic operation of the suction valve is provided: Operate the valve and check the funtioning is proper. Check that the correct operating procedure is displayed for the suction valve.

o Run test of EFP for 10 minutes.

o Verify that the relief valves are free of leaks and that water do not flow through them.

If the Emergency Fire pump is located forward and is located at th ebottom, then Means of escpae from the Emergency fire pump room bottom and also to lift any injured person to be provided. Check the arrangement and confirm it satisfies the requirement.

questions.prompt: Condition and Operational readiness of Prime movers.
questions.guidanceNotes:
For Diesel Engine Primer mover check:-

o Fuel tank levels - capacity is sufficient to run the pumps on full load for at least 3 hours.

o Valves in fuel supply lines from tank to the driver filter or pump system to ensure they are locked in open position.

o Lube oil condition using the dipstick on the engine crankcase.

o Battery chargers and batteries are fully charged and in good condition. Check the last renewal of starting battery set. (batteries to be renewed every 3 years)

o Check functioning of jacket water heater system (if fitted)

o Proper operation of engine instruments: rpm, oil pressure, temperature, amps.

o Exhaust pipe insulation and check for any exhaust leakages.

o Check sufficient reserve of fuel outside the main machinery space is available to run the pump for additional 15 hours.

questions.prompt: Condition and Operation of Bilge Drain valves in Em fire pump room
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check ventilation and exhaust / dampers & fan condition of the Fire pump room (obstruction debris / insects etc)

  • Check that the Emcy fire pump space bilge high level alarm is working good.
  • Check the bilge draining line has been provided with spring loaded valve (located in E/R) and it is maintained in good condition.

questions.prompt: Check and verify condition of manifolds and associated fittings
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check that cargo manifold arrangements and its associated fittings are in good condition

  • Check that manifold Pressure gauges are fitted outboard of the manifold valves on both sides are fully operational and have a calibration certificate.

Check that a valve is provided to isolate the pressure gauge, if in case required.

  • Check that all flange connections are fully bolted & bolt ends with atleast 4 threads out of the nut
  • Check that the manifold valves & lines are marked to identify the tank or tanks they serve
  • Check that the manifold drain lines & purge points are capped - drain line should be fitted with double isolation valve.
  • Check that manifold drip trays are dry and free of water. Check that manifold drip tray arrangements & gratings are in good condition.

Check reg the manifold drip trays are being drained properly to any dedicated drain tank.

questions.prompt: Verify condition of reducers, their securing and coating condition.
questions.guidanceNotes: Check the condition and inventory of manifold connection reducers for corrosion, flange mating face condition. Check thickness of flange for standard, are of suitable material and of same rating as the lines. Check internal condition for any internal corrosion or pitting etc. Check if properly stowed and secured.

questions.prompt: Check condition of the Manifold Hose rail & stopper plates at both forward & aft ends of the hose rail.
questions.guidanceNotes: Check condition of the Manifold Hose rail & stopper plates at both forward & aft ends of the hose rail.

questions.prompt: condition of Ex Proof deck light and fittings
questions.guidanceNotes: Check that main Deck light posts, ladders and light fittings are in good condition with no structural defects. Note - all light fittings in cargo area are to be ExD rated. (in case of Stern loading - lighting to be ExD for stern area as well)

questions.prompt: Condition of midship crane and personnel transfer basket.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check that the crane wires, sheaves, cargo block/hook & safety latch are in good condition. (Wires are lubricated with no signs of kinks, rust or nips. Wires are evenly stowed on the winch drum with no tangles or loops. Check hook swivel. Check wire rope terminators at hook and winch drum are in place and secure.)

  • Check if winches associated prime mover & brakes are in good order
  • Check that cargo gears are free from leakages with no oil accumulation.
  • Check that all limits are working in good order (Cranes must be fitted with hoisting upper and lower limits, luffing upper and lower limits) Verify if safety limits switches are tried out on monthly basis
  • Check if rocking tested has been carried out in accordance with makers instructions?
  • If crane is certified for personnel transfer, check the alternative means of lowering, if crane loses primary power. (this can include emergency sources of power or manual operation by hydraulic pump)
  • Check Personnel Transfer basket (PTB) - if provided - lifting ring, load line, safety load line, lifting sling and shock cords, aluminium rings line, splices etc. Inspect steel components for deterioration, cracks, distortion, weld integrity, rust etc. Check Tag lines are attached to the bottom of the basket.
  • Check condition of personnel floatation devices (PFD's) for transfer personnel & condition of Lifebuoy and line at he transfer location. Check condition of lighting arrangement.
  • Check that all cargo lifting equipments are properly examined and tested by competent person
  • Check when the personnel transfer basket was last load tested.
  • Check if Spare wires are available for Hose handling crane Check if One complete set of spare hoses are available on board
  • Check that winches associated prime mover & brakes are in good order?
  • Check that operator cabin/station in good condition?
  • Check that cargo gears are free from leakages? Confirm no Oil accumulation.

Check that all safety cut-outs are working in good order (Cranes must be fitted with hoisting upper and lower limits, luffing upper and lower limits) Verify if safety limits switches are tried out on monthly basis.

Check if rocking tested has been carried out in accordance with makers instructions.

Check if Spare wires are available for Hose handling crane Check if One complete set of spare hoses are available on board

questions.prompt: Condition of loadicator,Class approval certificates,Latest amendments,test condition results
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check loadicator software and hardware approved by Class and certificate available.

Confirm the Verification and comparison of the conditions with the Class approive dtest results every three months.

Ensure that the Verification and comparison of the conditions are verified andendorsed Anually.

questions.prompt: Condition of cargo tank domes, packings.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check that all the tank hatches/ domes, tank cleaning apertures and sighting ports are gas tight and maintained in order. There should be no corrosion, knife edging, signs of leakage of cargo/IG. Spaces served clearly identified and marked. Check Tank domes battening spindle & swing-out swivel bearing condition

Check if vessel have sufficient spare packings for replacement as inventory

Check vapour locks clearly identified for the space served & in good order.

questions.prompt: Condition of cargo tanks, coating and fittings
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check that the tanks are free from signs of buckling and structural members are in good condition

  • In case of vessels carrying cargo under heated conditions or cargoes such as naphtha, check for indications of coating breakdown at the deck head area especially the underdeck longitudinal.
  • In case of tanks having framo/deep well pumps, check the fixing brackets for the pump/associated pipe stack and the suction well.
  • Check that the bell mouth area is free from pitting
  • Check that the pipelines are well secured.
  • Check the heating steam coils and supporting brackets for wear and tear/corrosion and for loose securing bolts, clamps etc.
  • Check that the access ladders, handrail & guard rails are in good condition.

Check that the cargo tank high level & overfill (High/High) alarms are in good order and tested regularly.

Some vessels having the bonding wires for the level floats, check the condition.

questions.prompt: Condition of PV valves, PV Breakers and settings.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check that the cargo venting system is in good order & being operated correctly?

Check that the PV valves are clearly marked for spaces served and are in good working condition. Check if PV valves are inspected and cleaned according to PMS

  • Check that flame screens are easily accessible & removable, and are in good working condition
  • Check if PV Breaker filled in (Glycol) & provided with level indicator. Check liquid level matching with IG deck line pressure?Check that the safety relief valves have been tested, and test certificates on-board. Check if Officers are aware of the valve settings.

Check that Firefighting arrangement for Mast riser (if fitted) is in good order

Check that If stop valves are fitted which isolate individual tanks from the common venting system, they are provided with positive locking arrangements and that the keys are under the control of the person in overall charge of the cargo transfer

questions.prompt: Condition of Deck seal Pump and associated Pipe lines.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check that Inert Gas system including piping, Uptake valves, discharge & recirculation valves, PV Valves, deck water seal and loop arrangement and associated equipment including IG Fans, Scrubber water supply (pumps x 2), sight glass are all in good order. Check that system can maintain oxygen content below or at 5%

  • Check if maintenance has been carried out regular intervals including the non-return valve?

IG System - furnace, jackets

o IG Fans & bellows. IG fan water washing arrangements.

o IG Controls systems & panel. IG Alarms & Trips - testing.

o Deck Seal pump

o Scrubbers Units - Demister

o Scrubber pumps

Check that the instrumentation, alarm, trips, pressure and oxygen recorders are all in good working order

Check that fixed O2 analyser is calibrated before use, IG O2 recorder is functional and readouts are matching in value

  • Check that back-up oxygen content meter is fully operational.Suffiecient Calibration gas is available.

questions.prompt: Condition and arrangements of Pump room
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check pump room clearly identified & marked at the entrance. Check that pump room entry procedures are being followed by crew. Check that they are prominently displayed at the entrance of the room.

  • Check if up to date pipeline and mimic diagrams of cargo, inert gas and venting system are legible and in order are available in Pumproom
  • Check that handrail, lifelines, guard rails, fencing and ladders in pumproom are in good condition.
Proper Ventillation is achieved. Check if vessel has spare Impeller, Motor & coupling - in case pump room is fitted with only one blower.

questions.prompt: Condition and Operation of TC heater and fittings.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check Butterworth heater, end covers, associated steam and water piping arrangement, steam lines & valves insulation are all in good order. No leakage or soft patches. Main steam isolation valve free to operate and in good order.

Heater to have water level seen almost just above half in the gauge glass to ensure proper heating and no steam getting back to the engine room.

questions.prompt: Condition and functioning of HC detection system.
questions.guidanceNotes: Check that pump room continuous gas detection system/HC monitoring system & alarms are in good working condition & sensors calibrated according to PMS. Randomly check calibration of gas sensor.

questions.prompt: Condition of Bulk head seals
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check for any increase in Bulk head temp when the shaft operates. Also confirm oil level in the oil seal cans.

Check that bulkhead seals (bulkhead penetration glands) for AUS, Ventilator fans & COPTs are gas tight & well lubricated. Check seal pots in good order.

questions.prompt: Condition of fixed gas detection system for Tanks and Pump room
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check that Fixed Gas detection system is functional with no alarms- alarm set to 30% LEL

  • Check that Fixed toxic gas detection system (if fitted) is in good working order, with alarms set correctly
  • Check that detecting cabinet sealing in good condition.
  • Check that three way sampling valves are in good condition

questions.prompt: Condition and numbers as per requirement of Portable and personal gas meters and Span Gases
questions.guidanceNotes:
Confirm Vessel is carrying sufficient number of Portable and Personnel Gas Meters as per requirement.

Check the condition of Portable Multi-gas measuring equipments (capable of measuring O2 & % of LFL, CO and H2S) (Combined for use in both Atmosphere & oxygen deficient atmosphere) - in good working order? Check sensor, cable, hand pumps & extension tubes - in good order. Check if the equipments have been annually calibrated (For Bulk Carriers - Minimum 2 measuring equipments required).

  • Check Inventory - Span Gas (Combined gas, H2s-25 ppm, CO - 50 ppm, C4H10 50% LEL, N2 bal) for all Portable Multi-gas measuring equipment - ROB x 1. If Equipment is annually calibrated, then Span gas in not required.
  • Check spare sensors (at least one) & filters inventory for Portable Multi-gas measuring equipment is maintained.

If provided, Check condition of Personal multi-gas monitor (O2,HC, H2S,CO) - in good working order? Check equipments have been calibrated (Minimum 2 personal meters Preferred)

  • If Personal multi-gas monitor provided, Check Inventory - Span Gas (Combined gas, H2S 25ppm, CO 50 ppm, C4H10 50% LEL, N2 bal) - ROB x 1 Each

Check Aspirator pump (1 No) & Toxic gas detection tubes for Benzene (bunkering) available and within expiry limits.

questions.prompt: Condition of bilge system and alarms..
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check that bilge level monitoring and alarm system working. Confirm that bilges are free of oily residues.

  • Check that the bilge pump in pump room is in good working order and can be operated from a position outside the pump room
  • Check if Pump room free of evidence of leaks from machinery, pipe line, glands. Check for signs of leakage visible anywhere in the pumproom

questions.prompt: Condition of hydraulic power pack room, System, Deck hydraulic Pipes and Associated Fittings
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check that power pack room is clean and free of oil leaks

  • Check that quick connection to high pressure, low pressure and charging lines clearly marked, capped and kept shut

Confirm Anti splash tape applied around flanges.

where ever oil mist dtetctor/ level float is fitted on Framo pump tray, check operation.

  • Check that notices/procedures are posted for charging and air venting procedures in the hydraulic room.

Check deck piping - Low pressure, High pressure - main lines and branch lines & Pilot lines in good order. (No corrosion, leakages). Flanges & bolts in good order (SUS - not corroded). Check Resilient pipe clamps & flexible rubber element in good order.

questions.prompt: Condition and availability of hand pump for hydraulic valve operation
questions.guidanceNotes: Check that the emergency hand pump for valve operation is in good working order. Confirm operating procedure for the Emerg. Hyd.pump clearly displayed and crew is familiar with it.

questions.prompt: Condition of catwalk, gratings, railings and associated fittings
questions.guidanceNotes: Check that catwalks, walkways, gratings & securings, support brackets, hand rails &safety chains are in good order & not corroded, thinned down, or loose.

questions.prompt: Condition of decontamination showers
questions.guidanceNotes: • heck Decontamination showers & eye-washes clearly marked and in good operating condition. Confirm Heating arrangements or recirculation lines are provided for the system to operate during sub zero temperatures.

questions.prompt: Condition and operation of High level and Overfill alarms.
questions.guidanceNotes: Check that the high-level alarm system is kept POWERED ON during both loading and discharging operations. If the high level and/or shut-down systems can be overridden by a key switch, confirm that there is a written procedure detailing under what circumstances and by whom the system may be overridden

questions.prompt: Condition and Number of UTIs.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check that all UTI tapes & sampler (1 per tank being worked plus 1 spare - where UTI is primary means of gauging - ideally 3 nos) are functional and in good condition?

  • Check that the calibration / replacement has been carried out as per makers requirement.
  • Check that vapour locks has been calibrated.

questions.prompt: Check and verify condition of flexible cargo hoses.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check that vessel's own cargo hoses (if provided) are in good order (visual check for no deterioration / damage) and tested in accordance with PMS. Check that the hoses are marked with the test date and pressure, maximum working pressure & individually numbered for identification purposes? Check if appropriately stored when not in use, properly supported and blinded with at least 4 bolts.

Check the resistance of the Cargo Hoses and th evalue to be ................ ohm

questions.prompt: Condition and markings of cargo, vapour, tank cleaning lines.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check that the cargo lines, vapour lines and inert gas lines are clearly identified, marked & in good order and tested at regular intervals. Check cargo lines are marked with the date of test and test pressure. Check the joints between piping electrically bonded & bonding wire condition

  • Check pipeline drains and stub pieces valve are capped and in good order.

For Chem tankers - Check that cargo recirculation line with deck heaters & deck cooler arrangements in good order.

questions.prompt: Condition of TC machines, TC pump and associated fittings.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check that COW / Deck mounted Tank cleaning machines & associated piping are in good order. Check condition of anchoring devices for non-integral drive units.

  • Check that portable Tank cleaning machines & hoses are in good order. Hoses stowed properly and secure.

Insulation readings checked and in order.

questions.prompt: Condition of booster pump and associated fittings
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check Foundation, flexible coupling (to confirm at least one spare coupling onboard)

  • Check that associated valves are in good condition
  • Check the local instrument panel to confirm all indicators in good working order.
  • Check for oil level in the shaft seal system & conform no leakages.

questions.prompt: Condition steam line and steam manifold.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check that the steam manifold arrangements and its associated fittings are in good condition, steam isolation valves free to operate (not seized). Check that the manifolds are clearly identified & marked for spaces served.

  • Check that steam lines and flanges are free from leaks. Check that the steam lines are well coated. Check condition of main steam line isolating valve.
  • Check that Steam lines not in use are emptied and blanked when petroleum products loaded

questions.prompt: Condition and complete function tests of records of steam line pressure testing.
questions.guidanceNotes: • Check that steam lines are pressure tested prior loading heated cargo or atleast once every 6 months and records maintained. Indicate the heating coils not working, leaking or not holding pressure.

questions.prompt: Condition and Operational Readiness of Control Systems.
questions.guidanceNotes:
• Local and remote monitoring systems and temperature/pressure gauging systems in satisfactory operational condition.
  • Lamp test - to check all indication lamps on engine control panel are in good working order.

Pneumatic Valve operations satisfactory and vessel have sufficent overhaul kits.

Last overhaul of Pneumatic system.

Condition of turning gear arrangement, motor, gear, amperage, interlock, remote operating box.

  • anoeuvring system valves in good order - no signs of air leakage or oil leakages. Water/moisture from drain arrangements.
  • Shaft earthing meter, band and carbon brush condition? In working order?

questions.prompt: UnderPiston space condition and Scrape down analysis test results..
questions.guidanceNotes:
• Exhaust manifold inspection - condition of grids, manhole securing etc. (if inspected). Condition of uptake & cladding.

Check that Exhaust gas system & Uptake is properly insulated and free of any oil soakage. (particularly condition of laggings / blankets below GI sheet covering).Check exposed portion of exhaust inlet/outlet for high temperature using thermal image/Raytek gun (must be < 220�C, as per SOLAS)

Under piston space condition - liner condition, lubrication assessment, piston ring breakages & sticking, piston rod, stuffing box condition etc. (if inspected)Scare down analysis and results.

Fire extinguishing arrangement for UPS / Scavange manifold in good order & clearly identified. Procedures posted and legible.

questions.prompt: Condition and Operation of TurboChargers and Last overhaul date
questions.guidanceNotes:
• Air cooler manometer / T/C blower suction filter manometer - functional. Turbocharger Air filter condition.

Check Air cooler drains and water separator in good order.

Engine room with positive pressure.

  • Turbocharger Lube oil levels & sight glasses. Turbocharger Wet cleaning & dry cleaning arrangements in good order.
  • Cylinder lubrication system satisfactory. Check condition of Lubricators / Lubricator stations - No leakages, sight glasses in good order - not painted over. Vessels fitted with Alpha lubricators working satifactory.
  • Crank case inspection. Any abnormalities.(if inspected)

For Engines running at Slow speed, whether sufficient spares for Blower units are available.

questions.prompt: Operational Efficiency of Air coolers and condition of Tubes and Fins.
questions.guidanceNotes:
• Air cooler manometer / T/C blower suction filter manometer - functional. Turbocharger Air filter condition.

Check Air cooler drains and water separator in good order.

Engine room with positive pressure.

  • Turbocharger Lube oil levels & sight glasses. Turbocharger Wet cleaning & dry cleaning arrangements in good order.
  • Cylinder lubrication system satisfactory. Check condition of Lubricators / Lubricator stations - No leakages, sight glasses in good order - not painted over. Vessels fitted with Alpha lubricators working satifactory.
  • Crank case inspection. Any abnormalities.(if inspected)

For Engines running at Slow speed, whether sufficient spares for Blower units are available.

questions.prompt: Condition and Operation of Aux Blowers.
questions.guidanceNotes:
• Air cooler manometer / T/C blower suction filter manometer - functional. Turbocharger Air filter condition.

Check Air cooler drains and water separator in good order.

Engine room with positive pressure.

  • Turbocharger Lube oil levels & sight glasses. Turbocharger Wet cleaning & dry cleaning arrangements in good order.
  • Cylinder lubrication system satisfactory. Check condition of Lubricators / Lubricator stations - No leakages, sight glasses in good order - not painted over. Vessels fitted with Alpha lubricators working satifactory.
  • Crank case inspection. Any abnormalities.(if inspected)

For Engines running at Slow speed, whether sufficient spares for Blower units are available.

questions.prompt: Condition and Appearance of all Aux Engines.
questions.guidanceNotes:
• In safe & good operating condition. Operable upto 85% Rated capacity. (Performance must always be taken at max possible steady load at least to about 80% load)
  • Condition of performance recording equipment - engine doctor, lehmag, indicator instrument etc. In operating condition.
  • Engine well painted and clean. Check Shaft seal area for leakages. No leakage of Oil / Water / Salt.
  • Safety and alarm devices operational / function properly. Try out LO low pressure alarm & trips for engines.
  • Checking the condition of jacketed High press. FO pipes with particular attention of the pipe shielding, clamping from the high pressure pump to the injection valves and joints and couplings for absence of leakage/weeping.
  • FO & lub oil pipe flanges - FN tapes / anti-splash tapes in good order. FO leakage alarm in good order.
  • Means to isolate the fuel supply to individual engines - in good operating condition.
  • Remote control functions properly. Remote and emergency starting system working properly without air leakage, including operational condition of safety valves and pressure gauges, etc.

. Fuel oil filters to be protected to avoid any spary falling on Exhaust Manifolds( incident of Engine room Fire)

questions.prompt: Condition of Lub oil and Last renewal date.
questions.guidanceNotes:
• In safe & good operating condition. Operable upto 85% Rated capacity. (Performance must always be taken at max possible steady load at least to about 80% load)
  • Condition of performance recording equipment - engine doctor, lehmag, indicator instrument etc. In operating condition.
  • Engine well painted and clean. Check Shaft seal area for leakages. No leakage of Oil / Water / Salt.
  • Safety and alarm devices operational / function properly. Try out LO low pressure alarm & trips for engines.
  • Checking the condition of jacketed High press. FO pipes with particular attention of the pipe shielding, clamping from the high pressure pump to the injection valves and joints and couplings for absence of leakage/weeping.
  • FO & lub oil pipe flanges - FN tapes / anti-splash tapes in good order. FO leakage alarm in good order.
  • Means to isolate the fuel supply to individual engines - in good operating condition.
  • Remote control functions properly. Remote and emergency starting system working properly without air leakage, including operational condition of safety valves and pressure gauges, etc.

. Fuel oil filters to be protected to avoid any spary falling on Exhaust Manifolds( incident of Engine room Fire)

questions.prompt: Condition of Air coolers.
questions.guidanceNotes:
• In safe & good operating condition. Operable upto 85% Rated capacity. (Performance must always be taken at max possible steady load at least to about 80% load)
  • Condition of performance recording equipment - engine doctor, lehmag, indicator instrument etc. In operating condition.
  • Engine well painted and clean. Check Shaft seal area for leakages. No leakage of Oil / Water / Salt.
  • Safety and alarm devices operational / function properly. Try out LO low pressure alarm & trips for engines.
  • Checking the condition of jacketed High press. FO pipes with particular attention of the pipe shielding, clamping from the high pressure pump to the injection valves and joints and couplings for absence of leakage/weeping.
  • FO & lub oil pipe flanges - FN tapes / anti-splash tapes in good order. FO leakage alarm in good order.
  • Means to isolate the fuel supply to individual engines - in good operating condition.
  • Remote control functions properly. Remote and emergency starting system working properly without air leakage, including operational condition of safety valves and pressure gauges, etc.

. Fuel oil filters to be protected to avoid any spary falling on Exhaust Manifolds( incident of Engine room Fire)

questions.prompt: Condition of Alternators and last cleaning done for windings.
questions.guidanceNotes:
• In safe & good operating condition. Operable upto 85% Rated capacity. (Performance must always be taken at max possible steady load at least to about 80% load)
  • Condition of performance recording equipment - engine doctor, lehmag, indicator instrument etc. In operating condition.
  • Engine well painted and clean. Check Shaft seal area for leakages. No leakage of Oil / Water / Salt.
  • Safety and alarm devices operational / function properly. Try out LO low pressure alarm & trips for engines.
  • Checking the condition of jacketed High press. FO pipes with particular attention of the pipe shielding, clamping from the high pressure pump to the injection valves and joints and couplings for absence of leakage/weeping.
  • FO & lub oil pipe flanges - FN tapes / anti-splash tapes in good order. FO leakage alarm in good order.
  • Means to isolate the fuel supply to individual engines - in good operating condition.
  • Remote control functions properly. Remote and emergency starting system working properly without air leakage, including operational condition of safety valves and pressure gauges, etc.

. Fuel oil filters to be protected to avoid any spary falling on Exhaust Manifolds( incident of Engine room Fire)

questions.prompt: Condition of Exhaust Manifold Lagging and fire protection.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Exhaust gas system (Exh manifold, bellows, Turbochargers, uptake & cladding) to be properly insulated and free of any oil soakage. (particularly condition of laggings / blankets below GI sheet covering)

  • Local and remote monitoring systems and temperature/pressure gauging systems in satisfactory operational condition.
  • Crank case inspection. Any abnormalities. (if inspected)
  • Condition of Glacier filters. (if fitted). Check spare Lube oil filter cartridges available in spare.
  • Alternators well protected from water spray. Cable glands and cable supports in good order. Pedestal bearing sight glass. Alternator air filter & Ventilation arrangement. Check function of splash ring for alternator pedestal bearings on running generator engines.
  • Are all Generator mountings free of abnormal vibration?
  • Are all Generators bed free from oil/water?
  • Any visible Exhaust gas leak/improper insulation near exhaust gas manifold and Inlet/outlet of T/C?

questions.prompt: Condition of Lub Oil in the system and last analysis report.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check that following Cargo systems (Hydraulic / Framo) and equipment are in good working order :-

o Power pack - Main hydraulic pumps, jockey pump, filters, coolers, valve blocks & Hydraulic tank - condition in good order. Instrumention, local temp & pressure gauges, microswitches, alarms & trips - in good order.

o Check condition of hydraulic hoses of power pack system

o If power packs are hydraulic aggregate pumps, is the space fitted with oil mist detector, system operational and tested?

o Prime movers (Diesel engines/ Electric motor) for Framo power packs - condition & performance.

Regular purging of Cofferdams done and verify th epurging log to understand the amount of leakage where in acceptable limits??

questions.prompt: Condition of Main Engine lub oil and anlysis reports.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Record the values of Scavenge Drain Analysis and and check whether SDA values are with in limits.

Check the last analysis report of Main lub oil and confirm normal.

Ensure Main engine system oil is on continuous Purification.

questions.prompt: Condition of Aux engine Lub oil and Analysis reports.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check the lub oil analysis report for Aux engines.

Verify that the AE lub oil is on batch or continuous purification.

Check the centrifugal filters condition to ascertain the Purification efficency.

Carry out drop test of Lub oil to understand carbon and fuel oil contamination.

questions.prompt: Condition and Operation of Main and Aux Boilers.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check Boilers if in visibly good operating condition. No leakage of Oil / Water / Salt. Check that there is no steam leakage from the mountings, pipe lines. Securing arrangements of the boiler, foundations, rolling and ramming stays - condition good.

  • Safety and alarm devices operational / function properly. Try out Low-level alarm & cut-out, flame failure etc.
  • Boiler control systems- Operate satisfactorily from Local, remote and on automatic controls.
  • Local and remote monitoring systems - visual alarms/indicators, temperature/pressure gauging systems in satisfactory operational condition. Steam drum Pressure gauges in good working order. Last calibration date.
  • Check Boiler burner station - clean? Check condition of jacketed High press. FO pipes with particular attention of the pipe shielding, clamping from the high pressure pump to the injection valves and joints and couplings for absence of leakage/weeping. Condition of Fuel pump, nozzle, photocells, flameguard.
  • FO & lub oil pipe flanges - FN tapes / anti-splash tapes in good order.
  • Boiler Water level gauge gauges are clean and can the water be seen. Both local & remote gauges in good working order. Lighting arrangement for local water level gauges - functional. Gauge glass blow down drain is fitted in place and full length.
  • Gauge glass valve - condition of remote operating wires & arrangement for blow down.

questions.prompt: Condition and Operation of Boiler fuel and water control system.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check Boilers if in visibly good operating condition. No leakage of Oil / Water / Salt. Check that there is no steam leakage from the mountings, pipe lines. Securing arrangements of the boiler, foundations, rolling and ramming stays - condition good.

  • Safety and alarm devices operational / function properly. Try out Low-level alarm & cut-out, flame failure etc.
  • Boiler control systems- Operate satisfactorily from Local, remote and on automatic controls.
  • Local and remote monitoring systems - visual alarms/indicators, temperature/pressure gauging systems in satisfactory operational condition. Steam drum Pressure gauges in good working order. Last calibration date.
  • Check Boiler burner station - clean? Check condition of jacketed High press. FO pipes with particular attention of the pipe shielding, clamping from the high pressure pump to the injection valves and joints and couplings for absence of leakage/weeping. Condition of Fuel pump, nozzle, photocells, flameguard.

questions.prompt: Condition of Printers, Data Loggers and other Recording systems.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Machinery control room equipment and indicators, alarms, etc. to be kept in satisfactory operational condition. ECR - A-60 bulkhead panels clean & tidy, soapwashed.

  • Lamp test - to check all indication lamps are in good working order.
  • Checking the satisfactory operational order of the means of communication between the navigation bridge and machinery control position a. (electric phones, telegraph, public address system, acoustic tube).
  • Engine console, Engine Telegraph, Telegraph log printer, Alarm printer, Alarm monitoring systems, DC source. Console clean & tidy, with no oil marks & stains?
  • Data logger operational. Spare paper available? Printouts of logs properly filed.

questions.prompt: Condition of Insulation Mats and Floors in ECR.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Checking that the main and emergency switchboards are maintained in satisfactory condition, clean and well protected. Priority release groups identified & labeled. (identification / labelling / tags in place)

  • Main switchboard and other electrical equipment protected from water spray?
  • Check with Particular attention to be paid to safety protections, tightening and cleanliness of bus-bars, protection against electrical shocks, conditions of indicator lights and instruments.
  • Insulation mats are positioned OR insulation cement permanently laid up on the floor - in BOTH front & rear of switchboard & panels.
  • 1st, 2nd starting arrangement in good order - standby generator engine selection.
  • Are there any obstacles or unwanted articles kept on the control console or behind the switchboard?

Insulation testing of all electrical equipment. Records to be kept readily available.

questions.prompt: Condition and availabilty of Loose Lifting gears
questions.guidanceNotes:
Wire Slings & chain blocks well stored, maintained. Check if there are any damages, cracking or rust on chain blocks tackles and wire slings? SWL clearly marked on all lifting gears?

  • All loose gear, Pipes & plates - in store and internal spaces properly secured - so as not to move fall, collapse, or turn over due to ship's movement?
  • Last load testing dates for loose gear & report available on board.

questions.prompt: Operation of UMS condition satisfactory.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check that vessel maintains record of all alarm history while in UMS operation. Are all alarms received during UMS operations being logged in the ER log book or alarm record book?

  • Is this checked by the CE?
  • Alarm calibration and testing logs review.
  • Engineer's call alarm in good order and tested regularly.
  • Check satisfactory operation of dead man's alarm.
Check list of off-scanned alarm & confirm that necessary risk assement is available

questions.prompt: General condition of Engine room and Lighting
questions.guidanceNotes:
General condition of Engine room and Cleanliness.

Have names and number of each machinery been clearly identified, to prevent possible mis-operation.

  • Engine room Ship side free from visible structural defects that warrant further investigation?
  • All power transmitting apparatus / rotating machinery have protection covers and guards. Check in general gears, shafts, couplings, belts, pulleys, wheels have been properly fitted with guards.
  • All meters and gauges in working condition.
  • FO & lub oil pipe flange/joints - FN tapes / anti-splash tapes in good order.
  • Self-closing cock (deadweight type) on sounding pipes of FO, LO tanks and DB tanks in good condition & not inhibited?
  • Purifier rooms and fuel & lubricating oil handling areas are well ventilated and clean.
  • Funnel / uptake area - well lit and painted.

Engine room - electrical panel tops are clean & dust free.

  • Checking that the personal protective equipment such as safety helmets, ear-protection, eye protection, gloves, etc. are readily available in sufficient number and in order.
  • Are E/R personnel wearing hearing protection in high noise area?
  • If separate Tunnel - check tunnel closing arrangements, lighting and notices.

o Is there any abnormal damage on the sheave, swivel, and other rotary parts?

o Is the SWL of the crane and rail marked clearly?

o Are safety devices and limit switches in good order?

questions.prompt: Condition and Operation of Welding Machines and systems..
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check that the welding machine and the associated cables are in good order

  • Check that the welding machine is properly earthed / grounded (Ship's Hull is not be used as Earth return)Is the welding equipment in good order, earth secured, cables in good order?
  • Are safety guidelines for welding equipment, handling procedure and shock treatment procedures displayed at site? Is welding handbook and electrodes reference charts available?
  • Is full set of PPE (face shield, glasses, leather apron, leather welder gloves, leather jacket, leather trousers, leather spats) available & in good condition?
  • Check that the welding area has been marked and minimum 0.5 meters away from the nearest oil tank. Is welding curtain available in designated area and in good order?
  • Is the Welding area fume extraction system in good order?
  • Condition of portable point extraction fan (if provided) in good order.

Additional Welding Machines are available and condition.

questions.prompt: Condition and Operation of Oxy/Acetylene system and Cables.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check that the Oxy-Acy gas hoses and connectors are in good condition and no cracks in the hose. Check that the gas lines have been fitted with Flash back arrestors at workstation end (in addition to cylinder end).

  • Have the fixed pipes in the workshop been painted different colors for clear distinction?
  • Check that gas hoses have been divided into appropiate length and stored orderly?

Confirm the Regulators and Flash Back arrestors are renewed every 5 years.

questions.prompt: Condition and Operation of Flash Back arrestors and renewed as required both in Engine room and Storage area.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check that the Oxy-Acy gas hoses and connectors are in good condition and no cracks in the hose. Check that the gas lines have been fitted with Flash back arrestors at workstation end (in addition to cylinder end).

  • Have the fixed pipes in the workshop been painted different colors for clear distinction?
  • Check that gas hoses have been divided into appropiate length and stored orderly?

Confirm the Regulators and Flash Back arrestors are renewed every 5 years.

questions.prompt: Piping condition on Deck and deck Penetration
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check adequate eye protection available for engine room machine tools. Check that the Face shields are readily available near the machines.

  • Check that the Grinding and universal lathe & drilling machines have been fitted with Emergency stops which can be operated by foot pedals.
  • Check that the Grinding and universal lathe & drilling machines have has adequate lighting arrangement to sight the job & well lit.
  • Tool board for hand-tools organized, clean and well arranged. Check that no broken tools are used and kept in the tool board. Are all workshop tools fit for purpose (no handmade tools)?
  • Check condition of portable Electric & pneumatic tools, and condition of protective guards. Condition of electrical connections, hoses, insulations & joints. Any safety bypassed?
  • Check tool boards for Machinery - located around the machinery space in good order. All tools in place & identified. Tool board is clean & well maintained.

Check provisional lighting devices / hand lamps - are in good order - no broken globes, protection guards in place, duly insulated to avoid the risk of electrical shock.

  • Are safety harnesses / belts available for personnel working aloft etc?

questions.prompt: condition of Electrical equipments and portable lights
questions.guidanceNotes:
• Check that the Engine room store is clean and well arranged and maintained. Are there any unwanted articles & objects - which need to be disposed? Check no garbage accumulated in Store areas?
  • Check that all large loose items stored in the store / engine room are secured properly against damage during rough sea.

Proper inventory been maintained and Spare parts labelled and arranged.

questions.prompt: Condition and Operation of All purifiers.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Has FO & LO purifier performance check been done by shore lab? Check that correct size gravity disc is in use as per Nomogram.

  • Are de-sludge cycle time, feed rate, feed temperature and back pressure satisfactory and in line with recommendations on FO/LO performance check reports?
  • Is the purifier room clean, and are all systems, pumps and save-alls free from oil leaks?
  • Are the all back flush filters working correctly? Check counter entries in ER log book.
  • Are the back flush filters cleaned in an ultrasonic cleaner?
  • Are all fuel tanks being checked for water on a regular basis and drained long enough to remove sludge and heavy impurities?
  • Are all local gauges on purifiers and heaters in working order and reading correctly? Are they recorded in the ER log book?

questions.prompt: Condition, Operation and Type of BWTS.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Confirm BWTS in good operating condition.

  • Check system operable from Engine room (Local) as well as Cargo Control Room (Remote). Check HMI touchscreen and Data Logger - in good order. Duty officer familiar with BWTS operation.
  • Local and remote monitoring systems and temperature/pressure gauging systems in satisfactory operational condition. Safety and alarm devices operational / function properly.
  • Lamp test - to check all indication lamps on BWTS panel are in good working order.
  • Check BWTS system components such as Electrolysis unit / UV / Chemical dosage pumps, other Pumps, Filters, Backflushing arrangements, TRO units - in good order.
  • Check BWTS system piping and its components - in good order.
  • Check Ballast Water Management Plan & Record Book maintained - in good order.
  • Check calibration kit and sample testing kit onboard - in good order (as applicable).
  • Check ship specific critical and operational spares onboard. Check sufficient chemical reserves onboard (as applicable).

Verify any psecific requirements similar to difficulty in operation in resh water/ Muddy water etc exist.

questions.prompt: Condition and Operation of SCR
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check for proper functioning of SCR.
Verify watchkeeper's familiarity with operation on both automatic and manual modes.
Check for any abnormal alarms on control panel
condition of the control panel and fittings
function of preheater for catalyst
functioning of the local and remote level gauges for the Urea tank.
functioning of alarms/trips for failure of the SCR.
condition of the Urea tank ventillation arrangement. It should be clear of any obstruction or abnormality.
Check the area near the IP converters at operating panel for any oil stains indicating control air leakage from stuck/carbonized valves.
 Urea flowmeters readings are recorded in Engine Log Book.
Check the certificate of analysis of the Urea to ensure specifications are meeting with the Maker's requirements.
MSDS is posted near the Urea tank
Temperature indicator of the Urea tank is in good working condition. Check that the temperature log is maintained.
Record of entries for change-over from Tier II to Tier III and vice versa
PMS records of the SCR 
Urea and waste disposal records (as applicable). Recommended to maintain at least requirement for about 30 days consumption.
ROB of the Makers recommended spares such as temperature sensors, NOx sensor, dosing pump spares, preheater burner nozzle, automation spares.
Local procedure for manual operation of the SCR and change-over from Tier II to Tier III is prominently displayed

questions.prompt: Steering Gear System working on Auto and Condition and Operation of Valve Indication Units.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check Alarms ( Power unit failure & Overload) and automatic isolation system is in proper order. Verify every 3 months all the alarms is being tried out

  • Communication between the wheel house and steering gear stand is in order and working good.
  • Condition and functioning of talk back system.
  • Gyro compass repeater is synchronised with the bridge. Check repeater is visible from emergency steering operating stand.
  • Rudder angle indicator is synchronised with bridge indicators. Indicator is visible from emergency steering operating stand.
  • Emergency steering changeover procedure with block diagrams - has been prominantly displayed in steeing gear room in the working language of the crew
  • Steering motor and Auto-pilot unit - power through emergency switchboard clearly identified.
  • Try out emergency steering. Confirm when emergency steering was last performed
  • Local procedure for steering gear checks as per arrival and departure checklists are prominently posted
  • Local procedure for operation of emergency steering and auto-change over mechanism is prominently posted

Emergency Hydraulic oil storage tank (fixed storage tank with permanently connected piping) is fully charged. The Storage tank should be 90% full - or sufficient enough to recharge at least one power actuating system, including the reservoir.

  • Condition of hydraulic oil is in good condition, that lub oil analysis is done regularly.
  • Check th eoperation of Rams and the time taken for Hard port to Hard stbd and confirm operation as per requirements.

Cehck for any oil leak from the Rams seals.

questions.prompt: Condition and Operation of Hydraulic units, Rams, Accumulators , Tillers .
questions.guidanceNotes:
If Rudder greasing system is provided, check that the greasing is properly done and the system is in good working order. Visual inspection of rapson slide, and steering linkages.

  • Condition of rudder trunk spaces and condition of gland packing. If fitted, check condition of rudder bonding cable.
  • Timing to check for each pump from hard port to Hard stbd and recorded.

Greasing of Rudder Carrier Bearing.

Check the Clearence between Tiller and Ram at the slide block. Usually the clearence to be higher on top side and less at the bottom to accommodate the wear down. Also to check any possibility of Tiller touching the Ram if next docking is not due soon.

Check Auto greasing of Rudder carrier bearing is functional.

Check for Rudder Drop measurement if provision given.

.

questions.prompt: Condition of Man Holes, Securing bolts and weather tightness.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Ballast tanks Access hatches to check:-

Clear identified and marked, including hazard warning displayed.

Effective weathertightness.

Hatch coamings in good condition without heavy wastage or holes.

Condition of gaskets and clamping devises in good order.

Manholes, flush scuttles:-

o Covers and bolts in good condition without heavy wastage.

questions.prompt: Structural Condition of Tanks and Last record of Inspection.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Inspect ballast tanks/ Fresh water and check for:-
o	Bulkheads (trans / longl.), longitudinals, transverse rings, horizontal girders, other members in good condition structurally. (No heavy corrosion, deformation, fracture, wastage, kinfe-edging, holes or cracks).
o	Coating condition assessment - GOOD, FAIR, POOR (as per IACS definition)
o	Access ladders, guard rails, pipings & supports clamps / brackets in good condition. (No heavy wastage, holes)
o	Check the ballast system pipe lines inside the tank are intact .
Check the bell mouth area is clear and the shell plate underneath the Bell mouth is not eroded.
o	Check that the hydraulically operated valves mounted inside the tank are in good condition and there is no crack observed in the body. 
o	Check that the hydraulic connections are intact and there is no leakage of hydraulic oil
o	Check the condition of anodes, if fitted
o	No leakage, damages, deformation, fracture, wastage, holes or cracks.
 Corrosion of the internal structure of the top side tanks and inlets or outlets sea water valves due to the heat of upper deck and moisture in the tank
-���Fracture at the corners of transverse webs in top side tank, hopper tank and at the transverse brackets where there is no transverse web
-���Fracture in way of the connections of longitudinals to transverse webs, i.e.; at the cut out of the transverse web in top side tank and hopper tank
-���Fracture in way of the knuckle between the inner bottom and hopper tank sloping plating
-���Fracture in the double bottom in way of the connections of the longitudinals to floors, i.e. at the cut out of floors and at the discontinuities of the longitudinals
-���Fracture at the edge of the non-reinforced openings and manholes
-���Fracture at the connections of the deck longitudinals to the bulkheads
Inspection of LO tanks and checking:-
o	Bulkheads (trans / longl.), longitudinals, transverse rings, horizontal girders, other members in good condition structurally. (No heavy corrosion, deformation, fracture, wastage, kinfe-edging, holes or cracks).
o	Coating condition assessment - GOOD, FAIR, POOR (as per IACS definition)
o	Access ladders, guard rails, pipings & supports clamps / brackets in good condition. (No heavy wastage, holes)
o	Check the system pipe lines inside the tank are intact 
o	No leakage, damages, deformation, fracture, wastage, holes or cracks. 
Scupper pipes passing through tanks in good condition without heavy corrosion or holes.
Check for Sediments, residue and debris condition at the bottom of the tanks.

questions.prompt: Condition and Operational readiness off portable fire extinguishers in Wheel house.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Portable fire extinguishers (foam, dry-powder, CO2) - check number as per fire plan, condition of container/cylinder for corrosion / serious damage. Instructions for use legible?

  • Portable extinguishers - check operating instructions clearly marked & legible.
  • Verify last inspected by vessel as per PMS and shore servicing dates.

questions.prompt: Condition of Portable fire extinguishers
questions.guidanceNotes:
Portable fire extinguishers (foam, dry-powder, CO2) -

check condition of container/cylinder for corrossion / serious damage.

Instructions for use are legible?

  • Portable extinguishers - operating instructions clearly marked.
  • Verify last inspected by vessel as per PMS and shore servicing dates.
  • Check availability of spare charge(s). For extinguishers such as CO2 type which cannot be refilled on board, check for 100% additional portable extinguishers of the same type.

questions.prompt: Condition and Location of International Shore connection
questions.guidanceNotes: Check International Shore connection with standard flange dimensions available on board. Check - Four (4) sets of bolts & nuts, each of 16mm in diameter, 50mm in length + One (1) gasket available on board. Check that spanners of correct size are placed at location.

questions.prompt: Condition and Operational readiness of fireman's suit.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check the Fire man's outfit stored in the fire control station & other location: -

  • Are required number of fireman's outfit with SCBA on board? (Two (2) sets for cargo ships, four (4) sets for Oil/Chem tankers & Five (5*) sets for Gas carriers {*4 for < 5000 m3 cargo capacity for gas carriers})
  • Stowage condition in good order as per fire control plan.
  • Protective clothing, boots (electrically non-conducting), gloves, helmet, electric safety lamp (For tankers explosion proof type), axes (handle with high-voltage insulation) - are in good condition - especially suits not damaged / torn insulation / stitches opened / Velcro damaged. Check approval cert of Fireman's Suit to be in accordance with any one of BS EN 469:2005/2020 (Level 2 suits)/ BS EN 1486:2007 /ISO 15538:2001
  • Fireproof lifeline of sufficient length for each breathing apparatus with a snap hook.
  • Storage position is clearly marked & luminous IMO symbol.
  • Fire-Fighter's communication (Two-way explosion-proof, intrinsically safe portable radio) with charging arrangement in place?

questions.prompt: Condition of Fire extinguishing system, Pipe line and Nozzles.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Paint Store: Check that the sprinklers system (nozzles & piping) are in good condition and the sprinkler line operating valve is kept in good operating condition and properly marked. Check when last tested by crew.

If paint room is fitted with CO2 extinguishing arrangement - check bottle, piping and release arrangement in order, procedures available and valves clearly marked. Check that pressure test and inspection records of CO2 bottle are locally posted.

General inspection of Lighting, Light fittings, Emergency lights, stub pipes & cable glands:

  • No water ingress.
  • Bulbs & glasses without damage.
  • No corrosion of light fitting stub piece & flange.
  • Cable penetrating gland in good condition.
  • Lights operating satisfactorily.
  • Emergency lights clearly identified.

questions.prompt: Condition of Ex proof lightings and Ventillation systems.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Paint Store: Check that the sprinklers system (nozzles & piping) are in good condition and the sprinkler line operating valve is kept in good operating condition and properly marked. Check when last tested by crew.

If paint room is fitted with CO2 extinguishing arrangement - check bottle, piping and release arrangement in order, procedures available and valves clearly marked. Check that pressure test and inspection records of CO2 bottle are locally posted.

General inspection of Lighting, Light fittings, Emergency lights, stub pipes & cable glands:

  • No water ingress.
  • Bulbs & glasses without damage.
  • No corrosion of light fitting stub piece & flange.
  • Cable penetrating gland in good condition.
  • Lights operating satisfactorily.
  • Emergency lights clearly identified.

questions.prompt: Calibration records and condition of fixed gas detection systems & the sampling points.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check that Fixed Gas detection system functional with no alarms - alarm set to 30% LEL

  • Check that Fixed toxic gas detection system (if fitted) in good working order- alarms set correctly
  • If applicable, system is set to correct position sampling taking into account cargo vapour density.

Detecting cabinet sealing in good condition.

Three way sampling valves are in good condition

Onboard sensor calibration log & alarm testing records.

last shore calibration certificate is valid. (Annual as per company requirment.)

questions.prompt: Condition and Operational readiness of portable fire extinguishers in Wheel house.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Portable fire extinguishers (foam, dry-powder, CO2) - check number as per fire plan, condition of container/cylinder for corrosion / serious damage. Instructions for use legible?
Portable extinguishers - check operating instructions clearly marked & legible.
Verify last inspected by vessel as per PMS and shore servicing dates.

questions.prompt: Condition and Operational Readiness of Fire Hydrants, fire hoses, Nozzles, couplings, Gaskets and spanners.
questions.guidanceNotes:
General inspection of the Fire hydrants, hoses and the cabinet boxes fitted around the accommodation block: -

  • Fire hose boxes & stowage in good condition. Clearly painted (red colour) boxes. Check condition of fixing arrangement.
  • Marked with luminous IMO symbols.
  • Check condition & housekeeping of fire hoses, nozzle, couplings, gaskets & spanners.
  • Jet/spray dual type with shut-off device for all nozzles. Operation of easy change mode & shut-off devices.
  • Hoses last pressure tested - No leakages.
  • Spare hoses available on board.
  • Fire Hydrants - satisfactory operation of valves. No leakage / dripping. Fire hydrant end caps are fitted with pressure release hole and same is clear without any salt deposit.

No leakage, heavy wastage in lines.

  • No doublers, clamps, soft patches in lines.
  • Valve handles in good condition.
  • Fire hoses easily coupled to hydrants.

questions.prompt: Condition, appearance of Fire main line, Expansion couplings, drain valves & relief valves and free of soft patches
questions.guidanceNotes:
General inspection of the Fire hydrants, hoses and the cabinet boxes fitted around the accommodation block: -

  • Fire hose boxes & stowage in good condition. Clearly painted (red colour) boxes. Check condition of fixing arrangement.
  • Marked with luminous IMO symbols.
  • Check condition & housekeeping of fire hoses, nozzle, couplings, gaskets & spanners.
  • Jet/spray dual type with shut-off device for all nozzles. Operation of easy change mode & shut-off devices.
  • Hoses last pressure tested - No leakages.
  • Spare hoses available on board.
  • Fire Hydrants - satisfactory operation of valves. No leakage / dripping. Fire hydrant end caps are fitted with pressure release hole and same is clear without any salt deposit.

No leakage, heavy wastage in lines.

  • No doublers, clamps, soft patches in lines.
  • Valve handles in good condition.
  • Fire hoses easily coupled to hydrants.

questions.prompt: Condition and operations readiness for all Fire Dampers around the accommodation
questions.guidanceNotes:
General inspection of the Fire Dampers fitted around the accommodation block: -

  • Operate satisfactorily.
  • Clear marking of Close-Open No hole /defects on dampers.
  • Marked with luminous IMO symbols.
  • Wire mesh grid intact - in good condition.

Condition of Channels and visible corrsion.

Condition of channel Rubber packings.

questions.prompt: Condition of Wire mesh, channel and packings
questions.guidanceNotes:
General inspection of the Fire Dampers fitted around the accommodation block: -

  • Operate satisfactorily.
  • Clear marking of Close-Open No hole /defects on dampers.
  • Marked with luminous IMO symbols.
  • Wire mesh grid intact - in good condition.

Condition of Channels and visible corrsion.

Condition of channel Rubber packings.

questions.prompt: Condition and Operational readiness of CO2/IG cylinders and pilot bottles
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check fixed fire extinguishing system room is properly ventilated & ventilation system is in order. Check if thermometer is available to monitor the room temperature? Check temperatures are not high, A-60 Room insulations are in good condition - not adjacent to Fuel tanks. (CO2 bottle - safety discs may burst in high temperatures beyond 55 �C, for vessels trading in High temp areas)

Check that the CO2 cylinders are properly stowed. Check the external condition of cylinders for absence of corrosion.

  • CO2 cylinder have been weighed and services as specified by flag administration requirements.
  • CO2 cylinders have been hydrotested as per flag administration requirements. Flexible hoses renewed at least every 10 yrs or as per flag requirements.

Identify & verify the type of CO2 cylinder head valves/actuators if Type A or Type B.

  • The crew to be familiarized with the type of valve, the operation of the CO2 system and identify according to the manufacturers whether the safety pin should be in position or withdrawn.
  • Type A, the safety pin must be withdrawn/removed for system to be operatable
  • Type B, the safety pin must be in place for system to be operatable
  • Verify if the safety pins are withdrawn or in place - according to manufacturers instructions. (Especially vessels coming out of dry-dock or recently completed major repairs or Servicing)

Test of piping arrangement by air blowing. Check the discharge nozzles are clear and free from obstruction.

  • Test of microswitch and audible alarm for the release of CO2 gas functional.
  • Check Manifold pressure gauge reading. Should be Zero.

Crew Familar and can identify CO2 Alarm in the Engine room

There are other types of IG systems also used in place of CO2 System to have the same functions been done but with no dangers similar to CO2.

questions.prompt: Condition of the remote release units and test if alarm and shut down happen when the door is opened.
questions.guidanceNotes: Check that the operating instructions of the fixed extinguishing system are permanently posted - Two separate controls within a clearly identified release box - one control to open piping valve for gas, and second control discharge of gas from containers. (if release box is locked, a key shall be located adjacent in a break glass type enclosure).

questions.prompt: Condition and Operational readiness of hyper mist system
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check operation of Hyper mist system by activating th erequired Fire sensors.

Confirm the activation of alarms in Fire control station/Wheel house.

Check wthether the Hyper Mist system power supply connected to ESB.(as per SOLAS, this is not applicable to local application hypermist systems)

Check if a defect & engineering MOC be raised , to carryout modification in subsequent docking.

Availability of testing equipment for smoke and Flame .

All detectors attached to this system are functioning correctly and active.

All manual valves that are to be kept normally open are in open position.

Check FW level of the tank, confirm arrangement that th esuction for othere services from this FW tank is much above th eminimum tank capacity requirement for use for hyper mist.Temporary means to supply FW to pump from other tank using Non-collapsible suction hoses can be made available, which can be used when the designated tank is to be cleaned. Check that the suction piping from FW tank to pressure pump is provided with means of heating to prevent icing.

Tank valve positively open.

Main and Emergency source of power are in healthy state and available.

Audio and Visible alarm function for individually protected areas.

The pump is maintained well and in healthy state.

Manual opening and closing function of the protected zones solenoid valves.

questions.prompt: Condition and Operation of of SCBA sets
questions.guidanceNotes:
For Chem tankers / Chem/Oil Tankers - 3 IBC-SCBA sets (with protective clothing, boots, glove and tight-fitting goggles, fireproof lines w/belt resistant to cargoes & Ex-proof lamps) - 200% spare air cylinders OR - 100% spare air cylinders available on board + SCBA Air compressor. Check SCBA, SCBA Compressor & bottles in good operating condition. Check 3 Chemical suits available & in good condition. Verify that SCBA sets have been serviced by shore-based agency on annual basis.

For Gas tankers - 2 IGC-SCBA sets (with protective clothing, boots, glove and tight-fitting goggles, steel cord rescue line with belt & Ex-proof lamps) - 100% spare air cylinders available on board + SCBA Air compressor. Check SCBA, SCBA Compressor & bottles in good operating condition. For ships with Cargo capacity > 2000 m3 (14.4.4 of IGC), 2 additional sets, with 3 spare charged bottles for each of these sets are required.

For Container vessels & Bulk carriers - for compliance with IMDG code OR IMSBC (Group-B) requirements - 2 SCBA sets (with protective clothing, boots, glove, helmet and goggles, rescue line) - 200% spare air cylinders available on board. Check SCBA & bottles in good operating condition. (100% spare air cylinders if SCBA Air compressor is available)

Check the last Air quality test certificate and service certificate for SCBA compressor (chemical tankers). Check Spare Air intake filters & lubricating oil specifically meant of SCBA compressors are available in inventory.

Check BA air cylinders are hydrostatically tested every 5 years or lesser period if so recommended by the manufacturer. The hydrostatic test date must be stamped on the cylinder.

SCBA Face mask visers are clear and not faded. The operating valves are in good condition and valves operating in Good order

Audible alarm & pressure gauge for SCBA sets (bottle low pressure) - in good working order.

questions.prompt: Condition of portable gas detectors and the fittings
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check the condition of Portable Multi-gas measuring equipments (capable of measuring O2 & % of LFL, CO and H2S) (Combined for use in both Atmosphere & oxygen deficient atmosphere) - in good working order? Check sensor, cable, hand pumps & extension tubes.

The equipments have been annually calibrated (For Tankers - Minimum 2 measuring equipments required).

Inventory - Span Gas (Combined gas, H2s-25 ppm, CO - 50 ppm, C4H10 50% LEL, N2 bal) for all Portable Multi-gas measuring equipment - ROB x 1

Spare sensors (at least one) & filters inventory for Portable Multi-gas measuring equipment is maintained.

Portable Multi-gas measuring equipment has NOT been provided - check separate gas measuring equipments: Tankscope (capable of measuring HC in inert atmosphere), Oxygen meter & Explosive meter (for measuring flammable vapour concentrations) - in good working order?

Check Inventory - Span Gas (Tankscope, C4H10 6~10% Vol, N2 bal) (Oxygen meter, N2 99.99%) & (Explosimeter, C4H10 30~70% LEL, N2 bal) - ROB x 1 Each

questions.prompt: Condition of Personnel Gas meters
questions.guidanceNotes:

Eqipments have been calibrated (For Gas Tankers - Minimum 3 personal meters required)

Inventory - Span Gas (Combined gas, H2S 25ppm, CO 50 ppm, C4H10 50% LEL, N2 bal) - ROB x 1 Each

Aspirator pumps (2 Nos) & Toxic gas detection tubes available and within expiry limits.

Sufficient toxic gas detection tubes are available for Benzene (bunkering) & for Cargoes nominated to load

Dew point meter (if provided) in good working condition.

questions.prompt: Condition and Operation of Fire doors.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check Fire Door and closing arrangement. No hold back hooks, ropes or wedge to keep open self-closing fire doors? (If Fire doors are remotely operated - remote release operating good?)

  • Check condition of door, door frame and door hinge, lock mechanism being free of gaps between door and frame.
  • Check condition of Fire door packings and not subject to inappropriate cable penetrations.
  • Check Door locks functional and no handles missing? Check Self closing doors closing tight without need for human intervention?
  • Check doors are free of puncture damage to the outer skin on one or both sides of the door being free of inappropriate alterations or modifications.

questions.prompt: Condition, Appearance and Operational readiness of Free fall Life Boat.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check the Lifeboat EXTERNALS for the following: -

  • Secured and stowed properly.
  • Lifeboat hull inside and outside in good order - no rust, no cracks, no unauthorized repairs, impacts or damage to the hull of the boats?
  • Hull markings (Ship's name, No. of persons, POR etc) . Available on boat Sides and on Top of the boat canopy & legible.
  • Reflective tapes on hull. (Including bottom part of the boat)
  • Handrails / Grips / lanyards in good order.
  • Skates & Fenders on the boat sides in good order.
  • Buoyant Grab lines Beckett around the outside of the boat, on both sides are in good order.
  • Bilge Grab rails (where fitted), in good condition.

Verify the Kort Nozzles in Good condition and free movement.

  • if with rudder, Rudder stock, rudder, tiller and stern frame in order. Rudder free to operate - hard over both sides. (Sometimes the Hydraulic oil in steering system reservoir might not be sufficient or rudder may be working hard on the pintle bearing)
  • Door and hatches for tightness, gaskets around hatches for deterioration and those hatches operate freely.
  • Windows / door glasses are clear and visibility is good. (Not fogged). Check that lifeboat windows clean and not opaque or excessively scratched

Check whether th eexhaust flaps and free and in good condition.

One SART unit is placed inside the FFLB.

questions.prompt: Condition of Propeller shaft and clutch arrangements.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check the Lifeboats INTERNALS for the following:-

  • Check if emergency operation / manual operation of tiller available, in good order & crew aware.
  • Propeller and shafting with clutch. Propeller guard.
  • Stuffing box on propeller shaft for lubrication.
  • Instrumentation and engine controls are in order.
  • Fuel tanks are full and are free of corrosion / deterioration and leaks.
  • Engine start batteries are in order (terminals are tight and clean). Charging arrangements checked for condition. Check the last renewal date of the batteries. (batteries are to be renewed every 2 years)
  • Bilge pump with hose.
  • Plug(s) with packing attached with chain/lanyard & with PLUG indication of position.
  • Seat belts condition (alternate contrast coloring for differentiation)
  • For free-fall lifeboats - head restraints in good order. Securing arrangements between seats and lifeboat hull to be checked.

Check the condition of Compressed air bottles, free of corrosion, especially around the neck.

Check the pressure in the bottles and in range.

Emergency air supply system is in order. Check air bottles are filled to proper pressure.

Check pressure test record for air bottles

  • Emergency air supply pressure reduction controller & over-pressure relief valve condition.
  • Vessel has a high pressure charging hose from SCBA compressor to Life Boat air bottle.

check if the compressed air bottles had been last sent ashore for test.

  • General House keeping.
  • Ensure free access throughout and all loose equipment stored in its correct location.
  • Lighting, emergency lighting and signs inside boats.
  • Canopy lights (spare lamps available)
  • Checking the condition of on-load release mechanism
  • Operational instructions (water resistant) correctly displayed (inside & outside boat(s).
  • Is lifeboat compass bowl filled with liquid, card free to rotate, markings legible, operating properly & deviation checked? Check operation of compass light

questions.prompt: Condition of Hoses and last renewal date.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check the Lifeboats INTERNALS for the following:-

  • Check if emergency operation / manual operation of tiller available, in good order & crew aware.
  • Propeller and shafting with clutch. Propeller guard.
  • Stuffing box on propeller shaft for lubrication.
  • Instrumentation and engine controls are in order.
  • Fuel tanks are full and are free of corrosion / deterioration and leaks.
  • Engine start batteries are in order (terminals are tight and clean). Charging arrangements checked for condition. Check the last renewal date of the batteries. (batteries are to be renewed every 2 years)
  • Bilge pump with hose.
  • Plug(s) with packing attached with chain/lanyard & with PLUG indication of position.
  • Seat belts condition (alternate contrast coloring for differentiation)
  • For free-fall lifeboats - head restraints in good order. Securing arrangements between seats and lifeboat hull to be checked.

Check the pressure in the bottles and in range.

Emergency air supply system is in order. Check air bottles are filled to proper pressure.

  • Vessel has a high pressure charging hose from SCBA compressor to Life Boat air bottle.

check if the compressed air bottles had been last sent ashore for test.

questions.prompt: Condition and Operational readiness of Life Boat Engine.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Running test of engines: -

  • Checking that the batteries are fully charged.
  • Test engine starting on each individual battery. Engine should start immediately.
  • Running test of lifeboat engines for at least three (3) minutes. Ensure cooling water supply - or keel cooler cooled externally with fire hose.
  • Checking that there are no exhaust gas leaks, fuel oil or lube oil leaks.
  • Testing of engine reverse gear in both ahead and astern positions (Astern propulsion is available).
  • Alternate starting arrangements - hand crank etc.

Check the condition of the Radiator caps and its sealing.

Water Spray system, where provided, is in order. Check for spare impeller availability.

Lines are flushed with fresh water and that nozzles are clear.

Support / holders for sprinkler system in good order.

questions.guidanceNotes:
Check Life raft container intact (no damage to packaging and sealing), marked with : ships name, maker's name, serial no, last service date, No. of persons etc.

  • Check if correctly positioned on board, stowed, secured and clear of obstructions.
  • Check Retro-reflective tapes fitted & condition.
  • Checking the float-free arrangement (absence of obstructions or unapproved fastening of the life-raft)
  • Checking the proper fitting of weak link, and hydrostatic release and verify expiry date

Operating instructions are posted on or near life-rafts.

Checking that inflatable life-rafts are serviced at intervals not exceeding 12 months. If liferaft & HRU is type approved for extended service intervals of 30 months, Type Approval Cert to be sighted.

Check operation of Emergency lights near life rafts.

questions.prompt: Condition, Type and Operation of Sewage treatment plant System.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check if Sewage Treatment plant (STP) fully operational, including aeration blowers, transparent sight tube for air lifts & sludge return. Check transparent air lift tubes are clear. HL alarms are functional.

  • Check if STP Effluent testing is carried out regularly and recorded. Check test kit and ROB of testing chemicals good.
  • Check biological active dosing powder / chemical formulation for STP available on board and dosing carried out regularly.
  • When STP effluent is collected in any other tank such as aft peak tank, wash water tank, clean water tank, the piping arrangement and the tanks - same has been approved by the Flag/ Classification society?
  • If the vessel is fitted with sewage holding tank (SHT), Check if the flag/class approved discharge table available on board
  • If ONLY sewage holding tank is fitted (SHT), then does the vessel discharge this at more than 12 nautical miles from nearest land and is the vessel en route and proceeding at not less than 4 knots during discharge?
  • Check If a discharge from Sewage holding tank (SHT) is made, has it been entered in the ER Logbook and TE 046?
  • Is the Sewage comminuting and disinfecting system, if fitted, fully functional? & If a sewage comminuting and disinfecting system is fitted, then does the vessel discharge this at more than 3 nautical miles from nearest land?
  • Where Treated Sewage discharge is not permitted, is the overboard kept locked in shut position and not opened without the consent of Chief Engineer.
  • Check storm valves for signs of corrosion / leakage.
  • Is a sewage standard discharge connection fitted for shore reception facilities? Check condition of the flange.

questions.prompt: Condition and Operation of ODMCS
questions.guidanceNotes:
For tankers, Is the ODME type approved and does the vessel have the operator's manual?

Is the ODME tested as per requirements and test record available? Annual calibration records of sensor.

Verifying that ODME is fully operable and routinely used.

ODME - Testing of the installation, in terms of response time, alarms activation and zero setting/calibration, according to the makers' instructions.

Check & Verify entries in ORB - 2

Is there any evidence of bypassing of ODME?

Is there any memoranda or Condition Of Class (CoC) related to equipment/s listed in Form A or B

ODME - Check that the print out records is kept for at least 3 years and that enough consumable for the recorder are available.

questions.prompt: Condition of Engine room Bilge Pumps.
questions.guidanceNotes:
Check that spare sets of important parts maintained on board for the Bilge and Sludge pump & a spare Bilge Pump Motor at all time

  • Check that the bilge tank cleaned every 6 months & are the engine room bilges and steering compartment bilges and drip-trays, free of oil and oily residues
  • Test at least one E/R Bilge Alarm & Collect photos for bilge holding tank and bilge well samples
  • Check the connections between the E/R bilge pipeline system and General Service (GS) pump and/or sanitary Pump suctions effectively secured to prevent inadvertent contamination of the fire pipeline and ballast pipeline systems?
  • Check such connecting valves clearly identified and appropriately labeled to prevent inadvertent discharge of oily bilge into clean piping systems?
  • Check if the bilge and sludge systems properly isolated & seals fitted on flanges to prevent tampering? Randomly check EBMS Seal and verify the same with TE 037 & TE038.
  • Check if remaining capacity of slop and/or sludge tank is sufficient for the intended voyage
